questions.question: Which of the following is not a factory overhead cost?
questions.explanation: Factory overhead costs, also known as manufacturing overheads, are all the costs that a factory incurs other than direct costs. Direct costs are costs that can be directly attributed to a specific product, such as raw materials and direct labor. In this case, 'Manufacturing wages' are typically considered a direct cost, not an overhead cost.

questions.question: The price paid by an acquiring company is the
questions.explanation: Purchase consideration is the amount paid by one company to another when acquiring it.

questions.question: A component of an overhead manufacturing cost is
questions.explanation: Overhead manufacturing costs are indirect costs or fixed expenses of operating a business; that is, costs not directly related to the manufacture of a product.

In the dissolution of a partnership business, the net book, value of the assets is transferred to
questions.explanation: The net book value of the assets during the dissolution of a partnership business is always debited to the realization account.

questions.question: Work-in-progress is the

A.text: Value of finished goods on hand

D.text: Sales less cost of goods sold

questions.explanation: Work in progress refers to partly finished products. It forms part of the cost of production in a manufacturing account.

questions.question: Which of the following errors will affect the totals of a trial balance?
questions.explanation: An error in addition will affect the totals of a trial balance because it causes an imbalance between the total debit and credit columns.

questions.question: Subscriptions in advance is classified in the balance sheet as
questions.explanation: Subscription in advance (income received in advance) is treated as a current liability item in the balance sheet.

questions.question: Preliminary expenses of a limited liability company are
questions.explanation: Preliminary expenses are considered fictitious assets. These are not real assets but represent expenses or losses that haven't been written off during the accounting period.

questions.question: An effective acounting system should provide information

A.text: For promoters, director, labour unions and distributors

B.text: On internal and external reporting for managers and third parties

questions.explanation: Financial accounting provides essential information for both internal reporting (managers) and external reporting (third parties).

questions.question: One of the reasons for the existence of the public sector is to

C.text: Provide cheap services to all citizens

D.text: Take adequate care of the needy

questions.explanation: The public sector provides public goods and services that are meant to be accessible to all citizens regardless of status, thereby helping to correct perceived socio-economic inequalities.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of accounting information?
questions.explanation: While timeliness, accuracy, and completeness are qualitative features, affordability is generally a constraint or a cost-benefit consideration rather than a fundamental feature of the information itself.

questions.question: An ordinary share of ₦60 was issued at ₦76. The share was issued at
questions.explanation: When a share is issued at a price higher than its face value, it is said to be issued at a premium. Here, ₦76 is greater than the ₦60 face value.

questions.question: Deferred charges are also known as
questions.explanation: Deferred charges, or prepaid expenses, are costs paid in advance for future goods/services and are considered assets as they provide future benefits.

questions.question: The director's salaries paid are items of
questions.explanation: Director's salaries are administrative expenses and are therefore recorded in the Profit and Loss Account.

questions.question: Factory wages are always entered into the
questions.explanation: Factory wages are a direct labor cost and are recorded in the Manufacturing Account to determine the total cost of production.

questions.question: The authorised capital of a limited liability company is the number of shares

C.text: Approved in the memorandum of association

questions.explanation: The authorised capital is the maximum share capital that a company is legally permitted to issue, as defined in its memorandum of association.

questions.question: The chief accounting officer of a local government is the
questions.explanation: The Treasurer is responsible for the financial management, budgeting, and reporting of a local government, serving as its chief accounting officer.

questions.question: Work-in-progress is the

B.text: Value of finished goods on hand

C.text: Sales less cost of goods sold

questions.explanation: Work in progress refers to products that are partially completed but not yet finished.

questions.question: Departmental accounts are prepared to aid the comparison of the
questions.explanation: By breaking down accounts by department, a business can monitor and compare the performance of each management unit.

questions.question: The transactions relating to liquidation in partnership account is drawn by using
questions.explanation: A Realization account is a temporary account opened during liquidation to record the sale of assets and payment of liabilities.

questions.question: The gross profit on manufactured goods is the difference between the cost of goods manufactured and the
questions.explanation: The gross manufacturing profit is the difference between the market value of the finished goods and the actual cost to manufacture them.

questions.question: The cash basis of accounting requires the recognition of revenue only when they are
questions.explanation: Under cash-basis accounting, income is recorded only when cash is received and expenses when they are paid.

questions.question: One of the purposes of maintaning the account of a branch at the head office is to

A.text: Check the excesses of members of staff

questions.explanation: Centralized branch accounting allows the head office to monitor branch performance and identify anomalies, such as an unexpected 'excess' (or lack) of customers relative to results.

questions.question: A ledger is classified into
questions.explanation: Ledgers are traditionally classified as Personal (debtors/creditors), General (real/nominal), and Private (capital/drawings).

questions.question: Which of the following errors will affect the totals of a trial balance?
questions.explanation: An error in addition (casting error) within a ledger or the trial balance itself will result in the total debit not equaling the total credit.

questions.question: After receiving the financial statement, the Auditor General of the Federation must submit a report to the National Assembly within
questions.explanation: By law, the Auditor General has a 30-day window to submit the report once financial statements are received.

questions.question: If a partner pays ₦400 for 1/5 share of profit, then the total value of goodwill is
questions.explanation:
Total Goodwill = Value of share / Proportion. 
₦400 / (1/5) = ₦400 * 5 = ₦2,000.

questions.question: The purchases consideration that is lower than the net asset implies that the buyer has gained the advantage of
questions.explanation: When a company is bought for less than its net asset value, the surplus is credited to the Capital Reserve.

questions.question: The subscription paid in advance is treated in the balance sheet of a club as
questions.explanation: Subscriptions received in advance represent an obligation to provide future benefits to members, making them a current liability.

questions.question: The correct entry to reflect the receipt of cash sent by a branch to a head office is

A.text: Credit branch debtors and debit cash

B.text: Debit cash and credit branch current account

C.text: Credit branch current account and debit branch debtors

questions.explanation: The head office records the incoming cash (debit) and reduces the amount owed by the branch (credit branch current account).

questions.question: The founders of a company are
questions.explanation: Promoters are the individuals who handle the legal and practical requirements of establishing a company.

questions.question: The cost incurred by departments that support the production department with such activities as maintenance production control and storage are called
questions.explanation: Service costs are indirect costs from departments that support primary production but do not manufacture goods directly.

questions.question: The Act establishing the Institute of Chartered Accountants if Nigeria (ICAN) came into force on
questions.explanation: The ICAN Act of Parliament No. 15 took effect on September 1, 1965.

questions.question: Subscription in advance is an example of
questions.explanation: Subscription in advance is considered a type of accrual (specifically deferred income) as it relates to future accounting periods.

questions.question: The movement of goods from head office to a branch is treated in the head office accounts as
questions.explanation: Transferring inventory from head office to a branch is an internal 'issue' of goods, not an external sale.

Calculate the balance in the bank statement
questions.explanation:
Adjusted Cashbook Balance = 13,560 + 2,000 (Dividend) - 280 (Bank charges) - 600 (Standing Order) = 14,680.
Bank Statement Balance = Adjusted Cashbook + Uncredited Lodgments - Unpresented Cheques.
Alternatively: 14,680 + 5,120 - 2,300 = 17,500.

questions.question:
Use the information below to answer this question.
[IMAGE]
What is the adjusted cashbook balance?
questions.explanation:
Initial Balance: 13,560. Add items bank knew but cashbook didn't: Dividend (2,000). Deduct items bank knew but cashbook didn't: Bank charges (280) and Standing order (600). 
13,560 + 2,000 - 280 - 600 = 14,680.

questions.question: The process of using sales ledger balance to cancel off purchases ledger balance is
questions.explanation: Set off is the accounting process of balancing mutual debts between a debtor and a creditor.

questions.question: The current growth in the volume of trading and financial dealings in nigerian is helped by?

C.text: credit as a factor in business

questions.explanation: Financing activities are transactions or business events that affect long-term liabilities and equity. In other words, financing activities are transactions with creditors or investors used to fund either company operations or expansions. These transactions are the third set of cash activities displayed on the statement of cash flows.

questions.question: Earnings per share is a measure of
questions.explanation: Earnings per share (EPS) is an important profitability measure used in relating a stock's price to a company's actual earnings.

questions.question: Which of these is an item in the debit side if the sales ledger control account?

A.text: Transfer of credit balances from control account

C.text: Transfer of debit balances from creditors control account

questions.explanation: Only interest is charged to the credit if found on the debit side of the sale ledger control.

questions.question: The cash basis of accounting requires the recognistion of revenue only when they are
questions.explanation: Cash basis accounting requires the recognition of revenue and expenses only when they are received and paid for respectively.

questions.question: Responsibility accounting is particularly concerned with
questions.explanation: Responsibility Accounting is a system of accounting where specific individuals are made responsible for accounting in particular areas of cost control. In this accounting system, responsibility is assigned based on knowledge and skills. If the costs increase, the person assigned is held accountable and answerable.

questions.question: An increase in government expenditure within a year is taken care by means of
questions.explanation: Supplementary budget is a government financial instrument used when there is an additional expenditure to be made but was not budgeted for. Virement is the process of transferring items from one financial account to another on the budget.

questions.question: One of the purposes of maintaning the account of a branch at the head office is to

C.text: Check the excesses of members of staff

questions.explanation: The purpose of maintaining the account of a branch at the head office is to monitor and control the activities of the branch. This includes checking the excesses of customers, which refers to ensuring that customers are not overcharged or undercharged. This does not involve recording charges in shareholdings or liabilities, nor checking the excesses of staff members.

questions.question: Tola Ltd. acquired the business of Bola Ltd. and caused the separate existence of the latter company to terminate. This situation is best described as
questions.explanation: Absorption is the acquisition of another company by a particular company which causes separate existence of the acquired company to terminate.

questions.question: The main objective of accounting report is to provide information about ......
questions.explanation: The main objective of an accounting report is to provide information about a company's economic resources. This includes the company's assets, liabilities, equity, income, and expenses. This information is used by a variety of stakeholders, including investors, creditors, management, and regulators, to make economic decisions.

The founders of a company are
questions.explanation: Promoters are a group of persons who conceive the idea of setting up a company. They are liable for performing the formalities associated with the incorporation of the company.

questions.question: The officer responsible for ascertaining whether all public expenditure and appropriation are in time with approved guidelines is the
questions.explanation: The Auditor General is charge for checking if all public expenditure and appropriation are in line with approved guidelines.

questions.question: The profit of a branch is usually credited to the
questions.explanation: The profit of a branch is usually credited to the Head Office Current Account. This is because the Head Office Current Account is used to record all transactions between the head office and the branch, including the branch's profits. The Adjustment Account, Head Office Sales Account, and Branch Office Goods Account are not typically used for this purpose.

questions.question: The movement of goods from head office to a branch is treated in the head office accounts as
questions.explanation: When goods are moved from a head office to a branch, it is treated as an 'Issue' in the head office accounts. This is because the goods are not being sold or purchased, but are being transferred within the same organization.

questions.question: A non-profit making organisation prepares the using accruals, basis of reporting , the statement showing the organisation is doing is the
questions.explanation: Income and expenditure in a not-for-profit making organization shows the summary of all income earned and expenditure incurred by the company.

questions.question: The subscription paid in advance is treated in the balance sheet of a club as
questions.explanation: Subscriptions are incomes to the club paid by the members income in advance or prepaid. It is always treated as a current liability item under the statement of financial income.

questions.question: In a professional partnership, membership must be between
questions.explanation: In a professional partnership, membership must be between (2-10) e.g Banking or Law.

questions.question: Which of the following is an intangible asset and a measure of a firm's superior earning power?
questions.explanation: Goodwill is an intangible asset of an organization and the firms earning power can be measured by it.

questions.question: Subscription owed by members of a club is a/an
questions.explanation: Subscription owed is an income owned which is an item of current asset in an account for non profit making organisation.

questions.question: A suspense account is used for error of

A.text: Double posting verified in the ledger

C.text: Items that have not yet been located for lack of sufficient data

questions.explanation: A suspense account is used for items that have not yet been located for lack of sufficient data. It is a temporary account used when there are discrepancies in the ledger, and the exact nature or location of the discrepancy is not known. Once the discrepancy is located and corrected, the suspense account is cleared.

questions.question: If a monthly insurance premium is remitted to a company by the state government, the initial deduction from employees are recorded in
questions.explanation: It is recorded in the Agency fund. An agency fund is an assemblage of funds that one government agency holds on behalf of another government agency or asset.

questions.question: An effective acounting system should provide information

A.text: On internal and external reporting for managers and third parties

B.text: For promoters, directors, labour unions and distributors

questions.explanation: An effective accounting system is accurate, useful and timely. Its purpose is to provide information for external entities, such as tax agencies and investors, and for internal purposes, such as evaluating efficiency and profitability. The effectiveness of an accounting system depends on how it is created and maintained.

questions.explanation: Error of commission discuss when wrong individual's account are involved or when accounts of saome class are involved.

questions.question: Which of the following will a supplier send to a customer whose invoice was under cast?
questions.explanation: Debit note is issued when the customer has been undercharged where as credit note is issued when the customer has been overcharged.

questions.question: What type of stock valuation method would a vegetable seller adopt in valuing it's product?
questions.explanation: When perishable goods are involved, the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) is adopted but if the goods involved are not perishable in nature, the LIFO method is applicable.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common cause of discrepancy between head office and branch trial balance?
questions.explanation: The main cause of dispute between branch and trial balance and the head office is the computation of debtors balance and cash transit.

questions.question: The cost incurred by departments that support the production department with such activities as maintenance production control and storage are called
questions.explanation: The costs incurred by departments that support the production department with activities such as maintenance, production control, and storage are referred to as 'Supporting costs'. These costs are necessary for the production process but do not directly contribute to the production of goods or services. They are often considered overhead costs.

questions.question: The debenture issued at par above the nominal value is said to be issued at a
questions.explanation: If debentures are issued at a price more than its nominal value (face value) such an issue is called issue at a premium.

questions.question: The principal use of control accounts is to
questions.explanation: Apart from ascertaining total debtors and total creditors balance, control account also aids the location of errors with the ledgers.

questions.question: Which of the following is an accounting convention?
questions.explanation:
Materiality is a convention which states only stuffs which add significant value to the company should be taken into consideration.

According to SAS 1, the accounting concept are: business entity, going concern, periodicity, realisation, matching, consistency, historical cost while the convention are: substance over form, objectivity, fairness, materiality and Prudence

questions.question: Profit or loss in a partnership is usually arrived at after deducting from gross profit all expenses including
questions.explanation: Profit or loss in a partnership is usually arrived at after deducting all expenses from the gross profit. This includes interest on loans. Partners' salaries and interest on capital are not expenses but appropriations of profit. Drawings are not deducted from gross profit to arrive at net profit, they are deducted from the capital account.

questions.question: An instrument which allows public officers to increase expenditure within a year is
questions.explanation: An instrument which allows public officers to increase expenditure within a year is a Supplementary budget. As explained earlier, a supplementary budget is a budget issued to cover expenses that were not anticipated in the main budget. It allows the government to allocate additional resources to its departments and agencies.

questions.question: When a bill is negotiated to a bank, it is said to be
questions.explanation: A bill discounting involves effectively selling a bill to a bank for an amount that is slightly less than the par value and before the maturity date associated with the bill of exchange.

questions.question: The process of using sales ledger balance to cancel off purchases ledger balance is
questions.explanation: The process of using sales ledger balance to cancel off purchases ledger balance is known as 'Set off'. This is a common practice in accounting where the amount owed by a customer (sales ledger) is used to cancel the amount the business owes to the supplier (purchases ledger). This process helps in reducing the number of transactions and simplifies the accounting process.

questions.question: Show how the following transaction will be recorded applying the double entry principle: Rent ₦50,000 was paid by Mr. Roi to his landlord on 1st July, 20 × 7 by cheque.

A.text: Dr Bank A/c; Cr Rent A/c

B.text: Dr Rent A/c; Cr Bank A/c

C.text: Dr Rent A/c; Cr Mr. Roi

D.text: Dr Rent A/c; Dr Bank A/c

questions.explanation: The two account involved are Rent Account and Bank Account. It is clear that rent account received the value of ₦50,000 while Bank account are same value. Therefore, rent account will be debited and bank account credited with ₦50,000 on 1st July July 20 × 7.

questions.question:
Mr Ojo gives you the following information on 31st July 2017
Opening Stock 7,000
Closing Stock 12,000
Purchases 60,000
Expenses 4,500
Uniform margin of 33 1/3 %
You are required to calculate the sales
questions.explanation:
Firstly, convert margin to mark-up i.e, 33 1/3 % = 100/300 = 1/(3-1) = 1/2 or 50%

Secondly, Cost of goods sold = Opening stock + Purchases - Closing Stock
C.p = 7,000 + 60,000 - 12,000
C.p = ₦55,000

Thirdly, Profit = 50% × 55,000 = ₦27,500
Therefore,
Sales = Cp + P
⇒ 55,000 + 27,500 = ₦82,500

questions.question: Accounts can be classified into
questions.explanation: The division or classification of accounts are personal account (deals with persons, corporate entity) and impersonal account (deals with assets, revenue and expenditure).

questions.question:
Which of these will not appear in the preparation of control account.
I. Bad debts
II. Discounts
III. Returns
IV. Provision for bad debts
questions.explanation: The control account is a summary account in the General Ledger. This account is backed up by the details in the subsidiary ledger. The control account keeps the general ledger free of details, but still has the correct balance for the financial statements. In the preparation of a control account, Bad debts, Discounts, and Returns are considered. However, Provision for bad debts is not considered because it is an estimate of an amount that we might have to write off in the future. It is not an actual write-off.

questions.explanation: Joint venture is for a specific venture while partnership is for a perpetual succession.

questions.question: The cost of a machine is ₦10,000. The residual value is ₦4,000. It is expected to last for 4 years. Using the straight line method, what is the amount of depreciation charged per annum?
questions.explanation: The formula to be adopted is : (cost - estimated value) / years of useful life. (10,000 - 4000) / 4 = ₦1500

questions.question: The advantage of double entry is that

D.text: it is easy to prepare the final account

questions.explanation: Double entry is easy to prepare the final accounts, which helps in assessing the present position of the business, and how it has changed.

questions.question: The two recognised accounting bases are
questions.explanation: Accounting bases are the totality of method adopted by an enterprises for applying fundamental accounting concepts to its Financial transactions. However, the two bases are cash and Accrual basis

questions.question: Abelawo ltd operates a retail branch at Aba. All purchases are made by the head office in Onitsha and goods are charged to the branch at cost plus 50%. During the year ended. Goods sent to branch at invoiced price is ₦30,870, cash sales ₦13,020 and Bad debt amounted to 129. From the following, what is the profit?
questions.explanation:
Goods sent to branch : Selling price ⇒ ₦30,870
Convert mark-up to margin
50/100 = 1/2 = 1/(2+1) = 1/3
Profit = 1/3 x 30,870 = ₦10,290

questions.question: A fall or decrease in the economic service potential of an asset as a result of wear, tear and obsolescence is referred to as
questions.explanation: Depreciation is the reduction in the economic service potentials of an asset as a result of wears, usuage, tears and passage of time.

questions.question:
Use the following to answer this question.
Testdriller ltd, issued 100,000 shares at ₦1 each out of its Authorised share capital of ₦200,000 at ₦1 each. At the of the first call, all shareholders paid in full, except for two shareholder who owes ₦20,000.
The company's paid-up capital is
questions.explanation: Paid up capital is the total amount paid up or credited as paid up, on the issued share capital. From the question above, the issued share capital is ₦100,000 and two of the shareholder owes ₦20,000. Therefore, the paid up is 100,000 - 20,000 = ₦80,000.

questions.question:
Use the following to answer this question.
Testdriller ltd, issued 100,000 shares at ₦1 each out of its Authorised share capital of ₦200,000 at ₦1 each. At the of the first call, all shareholders paid in full, except for two shareholder who owes ₦20,000.
The Unissued capital of the company is
questions.explanation: Unissuer capital is the total amount which has not been issued out on the authorized, nominal or registered capital. Therefore, unissued capital = Authorized capital - issued capital which is : 200,000 - 100,000 = ₦100,000

questions.question: Every economic unit, regardless of its legal form of existence, is treated as a separate entity from parties having economic interest is
questions.explanation: The entity concept ensures that only the expenses incurred by/for the business, incomes earned by the business, assets acquired for the business and liabilities owed by the business are respectively recorded as expenses, incomes, assets and liabilities in the books of the business.

questions.question: Accounting standards are issued at the International level by the
questions.explanation: Accounting standard is a statement issued by the appropriate standard - setting body locally or internationally on a specific area or topic in Financial Accounting. Accounting standards are issued at the International level by the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) - formerly International Accounting Standards Committee (IASC).

questions.question: Retained profits is an example of
questions.explanation: Retained profit is part of the part of the profits of an organization not distributed as dividends but ploughed back into the business for expansion.

questions.question: The importance of bank statement is all except

A.text: helps to update the cash book

B.text: verifies the accuracy of balances of cash book and chequebook

D.text: helps to detect and rectify any error

questions.explanation: Bank statement is prepared by the bank to an account border showing the transactions between the customer and the bank within a period of time. It is usually issued monthly or at agreed intervals to the customers.

questions.question: The method of stock valuation in which the issue price is obtained by dividing the value of stock in hand by unit of stock in hand is
questions.explanation: Weighted Average Price (WAP): Under this method issues are priced at a weighted average price which is obtained by dividing the value by the unit of stock in hand. Issues will continue to be priced at this price until a new batch is received when a new weighted Average price is calculated.

questions.question: If only wages is shown on the trial balance, it should be charged to the
questions.explanation: Wages should be charged in the trading account only if shown separately on the trial balance from salaries. In such circumstances, the assumption, unless you are otherwise told, is that the wage is a direct trading expense to be included in the trading account while salary is an Indirect (i.e overhead) expense to be included in the P & L account.

questions.question: A statement in a double entry system in which are recorded all the transactions of one specific class, which takes place during the period is called
questions.explanation: Ledger is used for double entry book-keeping. It can be defined as a book which contains in a classified and summarized form of all transaction that have taken place with a particular person.

questions.question: Goods can be charged to branches using any of these pricing methods except
questions.explanation: There are three different pricing methods which are used for charging goods to branches. They are: 1. At cost price 2. At cost plus a percentage 3. At selling price.

questions.question: Goods were purchased for resale on credit costing ₦150,000 on 30th September 20X8 from Tosanwumi International. The entry to record these transaction is debit
questions.explanation: In accounting, when goods are purchased for resale on credit, the entry to record this transaction is to debit the supplier's account and credit the purchases account. This is because the supplier (in this case, Tosanwumi International) is owed money, hence it is debited, and the purchases account is credited to increase the total purchases. Therefore, the correct entry is 'Debit Tosanwumi International, Credit Purchase Account'.

questions.question: Transactions are recorded or posted to the ledger in line with
questions.explanation: Transactions are recorded or posted to the ledger in line with the Double Entry Principle. The Double Entry Principle is a fundamental concept in accounting where every financial transaction has equal and opposite effects in at least two different accounts. It is used to satisfy the equation Assets = Liabilities + Equity, whereby each entry is recorded to maintain this balance.

questions.question: A book of original entry that is used to record transactions before they are posted to the ledgers is known as
questions.explanation: Journal is used to record transactions before they are posted to the ledgers to purchase and sale of fixed assets on credit, correction of errors, opening of entries etc.

questions.question: Assets whose economic life exceeds one year is known as
questions.explanation: Fixed assets also known as non-current assets or long term assets are assets whose useful economic life exceeds one year.

questions.question: One of the advantage of Fifo is
questions.explanation:
The advantages of Fifo are:
- Easy to understand and operate
- No realized profit or loss
- Closing stock is valued at current price
- It is realistic
- Helps to avoid deterioration and obsolescence

questions.question: The accounting concept which states that financial performance of a company should be divided into accounting periods is known as
questions.explanation: Accounting period concept is also known as Periodicity concept.This principle states that financial performance of a company should be divided into accounting periods,usually a year.

questions.question: The fund set up for capital project and expenditure is known as
questions.explanation: Development fund was set up by the constitution purposely for capital project and expenditure.The fund is used to provide infrasturctural facilities.

questions.question: The similarities between joint venture and partnership include all except
questions.explanation:
The similarities between joint venture and partnership are:
- Aims is profit making
- Both involve two or more people
- Both have rights and duties
- They share profits and losses according to agreement or equally

questions.question: The receipt and payment account for a non-profit making organisation is equivalent to
questions.explanation: The receipt and payment account is a summary of the cash book. It is a statement of cash actually received and paid during a given period,receipts being debited and payments credited.

questions.question: An auditor who is appointed by the management to examine and report on the works of the various sections is known as
questions.explanation: Internal auditor is an auditor who is appointed by the management to examine and report on the work of the various sections/departments in the organizations.

questions.question: The uses of control includes all except
questions.explanation:
The uses of control account are:
- location of error
- aids management control
- easy detection of missing figures
- preparation of interim final account
- saves time and grouping of accounts

questions.question: The price paid by an acquiring company is the
questions.explanation: Purchase consideration of business is the system acquiring a business as a going concern by another business.

questions.question: The excess of income over expenditure is usually transferred to the
questions.explanation: The excess of income over expenditure is usually transferred to the balance sheet. If the income is greater than the expenditure, it is called surplus and it is added to the accumulated fund and when the expenditure is greater than the income, it is subtracted from the accumulated fund from the balance sheet.

questions.question: An account used to record differences in the trial balance temporarily until errors are detected is?
questions.explanation: Suspense account is an account used to record the differences in the trial balance temporarily until errors are detected.

questions.question: The dividend declared before the end of the year is known as
questions.explanation: An interim dividend is a dividend payment made before a company's Annual General Meeting (AGM) and final financial statements. This dividend is usually decided by the company's board of directors.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer below questions:
A company had an authorized share capital of #50,000. It offered applicants #30,000 share capital out of which the company collected #25,000.

What is the issued capital?
questions.explanation: Issued capital is the capital issued out of the authorized/registered or nominal capital. #30,000 shares was issued out from #50,000 authorized capital. Therefore, #30,000 is the issued capital.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer below questions:
A company had an authorized share capital of #50,000. It offered applicants #30,000 share capital out of which the company collected #25,000.

What was the paid-up capital?
questions.explanation: Paid up capital is a capital which has been subscribed for and fully paid for.The #25,000 that was collected by the company is the paid up capital.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer below questions:
A company had an authorized share capital of #50,000. It offered applicants #30,000 share capital out of which the company collected #25,000.

What was the unissued capital?
questions.explanation: Unissued capital is a capital that has not been issued out from registered/authorized or nominal capital. Therefore, Unissued capital = Registered capital - Issued capital = 50,000 - 30,000 = 20,000.

questions.question: The accounting entries for cash in transit is

questions.explanation: When cash are in transit, the adjustment should be made in the books of the head office.the procedure are: Debit Cash in transit account and Credit Branch current account.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions below:
Tade and Nkirun were in partnership sharing profit in the ratio 3:2. Their respective capitals were #4,000 and #2,000 and their drawings #1,800 and #1,200. Profit during the year was #5,400, before 5% interest on capital. No interest was chargeable on drawings.

What was Tade's share of profit for the year?
questions.explanation:
5% interest on capital:
Tade = 5% x 4,000 = 200
Nkirun = 5% x 2,000 = 100
Total ratio = 3 + 2 = 5
Profit for sharing = Net profit - Interest on capital = 5,400 - (200 + 100) = 5,100
Tade's share = 3/5 x 5,100 = 3,060

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions below:
Tade and Nkirun were in partnership sharing profit in the ratio 3:2. Their respective capitals were #4,000 and #2,000 and their drawings #1,800 and #1,200. Profit during the year was #5,400, before 5% interest on capital. No interest was chargeable on drawings. What was the balance carried down on Nkirun account?
questions.explanation:
Nkirun's share of profit = 2/5 x 5,100 = 2,040
Nkirun's current account balance = interest on capital + share of profit - drawings
= 100 + 2,040 - 1,200 = 940

questions.question: Which of the following is debited to the sales ledger control account?
questions.explanation: Debit note issued to customer is debited to the sales ledger control account.

Cash book

questions.explanation:

= 1/3 x 6,600

questions.question:
Use the following to answer the question
Cash book
[IMAGE]

The cash book closing balance will be
questions.explanation:
Compare the total of the debit side and the credit and find the difference.
Debit side = 6,600 + 3,000 + 2,500 = 12,100
Credit side = 3,500 + 1,300 + 2,700 = 7,500
Since the debit side is greater than the credit side, we have:
Closing balance = 12,100 - 7,500 = 4,600

questions.question: Rent prepaid by a business is shown in the
questions.explanation: Rent prepaid by a business is an asset and is shown in the balance sheet. It is an example of a prepaid expense, which is an asset because it provides future economic benefits to the business.

questions.question: The advantages of double entry include all except
questions.explanation:
The merit of double entry are:
- To ensure complete record of each transaction
- To prepare final account
- Helps to check arithmetical accuracy
- For useful decision making
- Helps to ensure control of the business
- To show the financial positions

questions.question: The cost price of a book is #9,000 and the selling price is #12,000. The mark up is
questions.explanation:
Mark-up = (Profit / Cost price) x 100%
Profit = Selling price - cost price
= 12,000 - 9,000 = 3,000
Mark-up = (3000 / 9000) x 100% = 33.3% or 33 1/3%

questions.question: When shares are fully paid it becomes
questions.explanation: Stock is the collection of shares or bundle of shares.When shares are fully paid,it is converted into stock.

questions.question: Goods charged to the branches can be priced using these methods except
questions.explanation:
Transfer of stock of goods to the branch can be charged to branches using any of these pricing method are:
- cost price
- cost plus a percentage
- selling price

questions.question: A document sent by the seller to the buyer to correct an overcharge is ?
questions.explanation: A document sent by the seller to the buyer to correct an overcharge is a Credit Note. A credit note is a document sent by a seller to its buyer, or, in other words, a vendor to the customer, notifying that a credit has been provided to their account against the goods returned by the buyer.

questions.question: Which of these will not appear in the control account
questions.explanation: A control account is a summary account in the general ledger. The detail that supports the summary account totals is maintained in a subsidiary ledger. 'Trade discount' is a reduction in the listed price of an item. It is not a transaction that affects the financial position of a company, hence it does not appear in the control account.

questions.explanation: Error of principle is a type of error whereby a transaction is entered in the wrong class of account.

questions.question: The elements of depreciation include all except
questions.explanation: Depreciation is the systematic allocation of the depreciable amount of an asset over its useful life. The elements of depreciation include the original cost of the asset, its salvage value, and its estimated useful life. Obsolescence is not an element of depreciation, but rather a cause of depreciation.

questions.question: Patent right and copy right are classified as
questions.explanation: Patent right and copyright are examples of intangible assets. Intangible assets are non-physical assets that have a monetary value since they represent potential revenue. Intangible assets include patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade names, franchise licenses, government licenses, goodwill, and other items that are distinct and confer specific rights to their owners.

questions.question: Staplers, perforators and waste baskets are expected to be used for more than one accounting period and should be measured over the period of use regardless of their insignificant costs. The appropriate applicable concept to the above is
questions.explanation: Materiality concept states that all expenditure incurred to generate revenue will be matched against the revenue generated to determine the net income or loss.

questions.explanation: The sources of packages are: - Manufacturer - Computer bureaus - Software firms - Government - Research organizations

questions.question:
The following information relates to questions 7 and 8. The invoice price of an inventory was N90,000 with a trade discount of 5%. It was issued by Mohammed to Chukwudi.

In what book will the transaction be recorded by Chukwudi?
questions.explanation: Purchase day book is the book of original entry for recording all the goods purchased on credit from suppliers.

questions.question:
The following information relates to questions 7 and 8. The invoice price of an inventory was N90,000 with a trade discount of 5%. It was issued by Mohammed to Chukwudi.
How much will be recorded by Mohammed and in what book of original entry?
questions.explanation: The invoice price of an inventory was N90,000 with a trade discount of 5%. The trade discount is deducted from the invoice price to get the amount that will be recorded by Mohammed. Therefore, N90,000 - 5% of N90,000 = N85,500. This transaction will be recorded in the sales day book because Mohammed is the seller.

questions.question: In preparing the manufacturing account, if the market value of cost of production is higher than the actual cost of production, the difference represents
questions.explanation: Manufacturing profit is the excess of transfer value of goods manufacture over their actual production cost.

questions.question:
Mensah Enterprises sold goods worth GH₵850,000 to Isaiah subject to a trade discount of 15% and cash discount of 10%.
Calculate the net sales value
questions.explanation: Invoice price = 850,000. Less: 15% Trade discount ( 15% x 850,000)= 127,500. Net Sales value = 850,000 - 127,500 = 722,500

questions.question:
Mensah Enterprises sold goods worth GH₵850,000 to Isaiah subject to a trade discount of 15% and cash discount of 10%.
Calculate the amount received by Mensah Enterprises
questions.explanation: Net Sales value = 722,500. Cash Discount = (10% x 722,500) = 72,250. Amount received = 722,500 - 72,250 = 650,250

questions.question: Which of the following user groups require the most detailed financial information?
questions.explanation: It helps managers make operational decisions-intended to help increase the company's operational efficiency while also helps in making lon-term investment decisions.

questions.explanation: The accounting entries are: Dr Trade receivable account; Cr Irrecoverable debts account.

questions.question:
Use the information below to answer question 16.
The following information was extracted from the cash book of Mohammed for the year ended 31 December, 2018.
| | N'000 |
| :--- | :---: |
| Balance b/d | 5180 |
| Bank charges | 20 |
| Standing order | 560 |
| Dishonoured cheques | 85 |
| Direct credit | 600 |

Calculate the closing balance on his adjusted cash book
questions.explanation: Adjusted Cash Book: Dr side = Balance b/d (5180) + Direct credit (600) = 5780. Cr side = Bank charges (20) + Standing order (560) + Dishonoured cheque (85) = 665. Balance c/d = 5780 - 665 = 5115. Note: UI answer B (5285) matches 5180 + 600 - 20 - 475. Recalculation: 5180 + 600 - 20 - 560 + 85? No. Standard: 5180 + 600 - 20 - 560 - 85 = 5115. UI marked B as correct which is 5285.

questions.question: The relationship between a company and the external party is defined by which of the following documents?
questions.explanation: Memorandum of Association is the constitution of a company, which governs the relationship of a company with the outside world.

questions.explanation: Prime Cost = Cost of raw materials (9000) + Manufacturing wages (5200) + Payment of royalties (1600) = GH₵ 15,800

questions.question:

| | GH₵ |

| Work in progress 31/12/2019 | 600 |
| Closing inventories of finished goods | 900 |
| Manufacturing goods transferred to Sales department at cost plus | 25% |

- Calculate the manufacturing profit/loss
questions.explanation: Cost of Production = Prime Cost (15800) + Overheads (4600) + (Opening WIP 1600 - Closing WIP 600) = 15800 + 4600 + 1000 = 21,400. Manufacturing Profit = 25% of Cost of Production = 0.25 x 21,400 = GH₵ 5,350.

questions.question: A suspense account is used when

A.text: The errors do not affect the trial balance

C.text: The amount involved is not material

D.text: The amount involved is quite significant

questions.explanation: A suspense account is used to record differences in the trial balance temporarily until errors are detected.

questions.question: The full meaning of the acronym "IASB" within accounting regulatory framework is
questions.explanation: International Accounting Standards Board is an independent, private-sector body that develops and approves International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS).

questions.question: In which year did the Italian Monk, Luca Pacioli publishes his text on book-keeping?
questions.explanation: Luca Pacioli published his text on book-keeping in the year 1494.

questions.question:

| :--- | :---: |
| Trade receivables control account | 1000 |
| Provision for doubtful debts | 100 |
Provision for discount allowed on Receivables is 5%

Calculate the amount of discount allowed
questions.explanation: Allowance for discount allowed = (Receivables - Prov. for doubtful debts) x 5% = (1000 - 100) x 5% = 45. In L$'000, this is L$45,000.

questions.explanation: Net Receivables = Gross Receivables (1000) - Prov. for doubtful debts (100) - Prov for discount (45) = 855. In L$'000, this is L$855,000.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true of a sole proprietorship?

A.text: He takes decisions quickly without consulting anybody

B.text: He is highly committed because the profit is entirely his own

C.text: It is regulated by special rule of laws

D.text: The individual provides the capital and employs a handful of people, if and when necessary

questions.explanation: Sole proprietorships are generally easy to establish and not heavily regulated by special rules of law compared to companies.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of revenue expenditure?
questions.explanation: Revenue/Recurrent expenditure are expenses used for the day to day running of the activities of government/business. Petrol costs for a van is a recurring running cost.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reason why cash books and ledger accounts need to be balanced?

A.text: Determine how much the business owe other people

B.text: Established how much was received from or spent on various business activities.

C.text: Ascertain how much is stole from the business

D.text: To find how much is left in the bank account

questions.explanation: Accounting records are designed to reflect legitimate transactions; they aren't inherently used to calculate stolen amounts directly through standard balancing.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a cause of depreciation of noncurrent assets?
questions.explanation: Depreciation of noncurrent assets is caused by factors such as physical deterioration, depletion, and effluxion of time. Deflation refers to general price level changes.

questions.question: Mr. Koffi, in partnership with Mr. Nana, withdrew GH₵100,000 from the partnership account for his personal use. The amount should be:
questions.explanation: When an individual or a partner withdrew for personal use, it is treated as drawings and debited to the current account in partnership accounting.

questions.question: In which of the following is a transaction first recorded?
questions.explanation: Journal is a day-to-day book of business transactions where both aspects of all transactions are recorded first in chronological order.

questions.question: Mensah and John are in partnership without any written agreement. Their capital is in the ratio 2:1 respectively. John advanced a loan of GH₵200,000 to the partnership in excess of his capital. Profit for the first year was GH₵150,000. - How much is Mensah's share of the profit?
questions.explanation: Since there is no written agreement between the partners, the profit will be shared equally: 150,000 / 2 = 75,000.

questions.question: When a photocopy machine is purchased on credit for N500,000, state the account to be debited and credited
questions.explanation: Office Equipment (Asset) is debited because it increased, and the Seller (Liability) is credited because the obligation to pay increased.

questions.question: The following accounting services are performed by members in practice, EXCEPT
questions.explanation: Treasury management is typically an internal function of a company, rather than a service provided by external accountants in public practice.

questions.question: If Kessellie allows his building worth L$300,000 to be used for the business then, the
questions.explanation: An owner's investment into the company will increase the company's assets (building) and equity. Assets will increase by L$300,000.

questions.question: The excess of opening capital over closing capital represents
questions.explanation: If opening capital is greater than closing capital (assuming no drawings or additional capital), it represents a net loss.

- Calculate the accumulated depreciation on the generator as at 31 December 2016, using the straight line method.

- Calculate the gain or loss on disposal
questions.explanation: Carrying value = Cost (500,000) - Accumulated Dep. (140,000) = 360,000. Sale Proceeds = 250,000. Loss on disposal = 360,000 - 250,000 = 110,000.

questions.question: In a situation of incomplete records, profit is determined as
questions.explanation: In a single entry, profit is calculated as : NP = CC+ D - OC - AC where: OC = opening capital AC= Additional capital NP= Net profit D = drawings CC= Closing capital

questions.question: An office equipment bought for use was found to be defective and returned to the supplier. The subsidiary book to record this transaction is
questions.explanation: Returns outward book is used for recording goods returned to supplier by the business. Goods can be returned as a result of damages or defectiveness.

questions.question: When bank charges are discovered in a bank statement, the adjustment is effected in the
questions.explanation: Bank charges are the various fees account holders are charged in respect of maintenance of the account along with any other charges incurred in respect of specific transactions. These charges are usually not recorded by the bank statement at the end of a month which is why balance as per bank statement may be lower than the cash book balance. The difference needs to be eliminating by adjusting the cash book of the company before preparing bank reconciliation.

questions.question: Which of these is not a method of stock valuation ?
questions.explanation: The methods of stock valuation include Weighted Average Price (WAP), Standard Average Price (SAP), and Base. However, NAP is not a recognized method of stock valuation.

questions.question: A trader adds 25% on cost as profit. The profit on sales of $300,000 would be
questions.explanation: The profit is calculated as a percentage of the cost. If the trader adds 25% on cost as profit, it means the cost price represents 100% and the selling price represents 125% (100% cost + 25% profit). Therefore, the profit on sales of $300,000 would be 25% of the selling price. This can be calculated as (25/125) * 300,000 = $60,000. Hence, the correct option is $60,000.

questions.question: A debit entry in a fixed asset account represents

A.text: a decrease in the fixed asset account

B.text: a profit on disposal of the fixed asset

C.text: a loss on disposal of the fixed asset

D.text: an increase in the fixed asset account

questions.explanation: A debit entry is an accounting entry that results in either an increase in assets or a decrease in liabilities on a company's balance sheet.

questions.explanation: Trading account is constructed for the purpose of finding the gross profit or los of a business at a particular period.It is a revenue account and form part of the double entry system.

questions.question: A non-cash expense chargeable against profit and loss account is
questions.explanation: The provision for doubtful debts is an estimated amount of bad debts that are likely to arise from the accounts receivable that have been given but not yet collected from the debtors.

questions.explanation: Direct expenses are those costs that do not relate to direct materials or labour but are incurred specifically on a particular product and on every unit produced e.g royalty, excise duty, franchise etc

questions.question: Accumulated fund of a club is equivalent to its
questions.explanation: Accumulated fund held by a non-profit making organization to which a surplus of income over expenditure is credited and to which any deficit is debited is equivalent to capital. Since financial assets minus outstanding liabilities equal net financial assets, net worth can also be conveniently expressed as non-financial assets plus net financial assets.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions 21, 22 and 23
Ata, Bubu and Chikum were in partnership sharing profits and losses in proportion to their capital contributions:

| | Capital (#) | Drawings (#) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: |
| Ata | 40000 | 8000 |
| Bubu | 30000 | 5000 |
| Chikum | 20000 | - |

Net profit for the year was #40,500 and the interest on capital was 5% per annum
The profit available for sharing by the partners is
questions.explanation:
Interest on capital : 5%
Ata : 5% x 40,000 = 2,000
Bubu: 5% x 30,000 = 1,500
Chikum : 5% x 20,000 = 1,000
Total partners profit = Net profit - Interest on capital = 40,500 - (2,000+ 1,500 + 1,000) = 40,500 - 4,500 = 36,000

questions.question: Use the information in question 21 to answer this question. The balance in Chikum's Current Account is
questions.explanation:
Total ratio = proportion of capital contribution i.e 4 +3+2 = 9
Total partners share of profit = 36,000
Ata = 4/9 x 36,000 = 16,000
Bubu = 3/9 x 36,000 = 12,000
Chi = 2/9 x 36,000= 8,000
Chikum's current capital = Interest on capital + share of profit = 1000 + 8000 = 9,000

questions.question: Use the information in question 21 to answer this question. Bubu's share of profit is
The document which serves as the authority to incur expenditure in public sector is
questions.explanation: Warrant is the instrument issued by the minister who authorized an officer to incur expenditure. Expenditure cannot be incurred without the use of warrant.

questions.question: The double entry for the interest on drawings by a partner is: debit

A.text: Appropriation Account; credit Partner's Current Account

B.text: Profit and loss Account; credit Interest Account

C.text: Partner's Current Account; credit Appropriation Account

D.text: Interest Account; credit Profit and Loss Account

questions.explanation: Interest on drawing is introduced to prevent the partners from withdrawing cash unnecessarily from the business. It is debited to the current account.

questions.question: A partner who contributes capital but does not participate in the day-to-day running of the business is
questions.explanation: A sleeping partner also known Dormant partner is a partner who contributes capital but not participate actively in the running of the business; he has contributed financially but takes no part in the administration and management of the firm.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions below:

The cost of sales for Sportwear Department was
questions.explanation: Cost of sales of sport wear = opening stock + purchase + wages - closing stock = 11,400 + 80,000 + 7,600 - 15,200 = 83,800

questions.question: A business extracted its trial balance and discovered that the total of the credit side exceeded the total of the debit side. Pending further investigation, the difference would be

C.text: debited to Profit and Loss Account

D.text: credited to Profit and Loss Account

questions.explanation: When the credit side of the trial balance is greater than the debit side, a suspense account will be opened and debited with the difference.

questions.question: Recognition of profit when goods are sold and the buyer takes ownership of them is line with
questions.explanation: Realization concept states that income is considered or regarded to have been earned when goods are dispatched to the customer and he incurs liability for them.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer the questions 30 and 31:
A manufacturing company's cost of production was D 200,000. The finished goods were transferred to the warehouse at D 220,000. At the end of the year, 9% of these goods were still in stock.

The value of closing stock of finished goods in the trading account is
questions.explanation: The value of the closing stock of finished goods in the trading account is calculated as 9% of the cost at which the goods were transferred to the warehouse. Therefore, the value of the closing stock is 9% of D 220,000 which is D 19,800.

questions.question: Use the information in question 30 to answer this question. The value of closing stock of finished goods that would be shown in the balance sheet is
questions.explanation: The value of the closing stock of finished goods that would be shown in the balance sheet is calculated as 9% of the cost at which the goods were transferred to the warehouse. Therefore, the value of the closing stock is 9% of D 220,000 which is D 19,800.

questions.question: An example of real account is
questions.explanation: Real account are accounts of tangible assets, physical assets or properties e.g motor vehicle, land, cash, furniture,plant and machinery, stock.

questions.question: Eze introduces his private car into his business. The aspect of accounting equation of the business that would be affected are
questions.explanation: When an asset is introduced into business, the assets and capital will be affected i.e there will be an increase in assets and capital.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions 34, 35 and 36:
Receipts and payments Account of Abaluare Youth Club for the year ended 31st December 2018
| | # | | # |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Balance b/f | 650000 | Wages and salaries | 2400000 |
| Subscriptions | 9660000 | Rent and rates | 600000 |
| Annual dance tickets | 1500000 | Anniversary dance expenses | 955000 |
| Donations | 800000 | Travelling expenses | 140000 |
| | | Kitchen equipment | 4000000 |
| | | Balance c/d | 4515000 |
| | **12610000** | | **12610000** |

Subscriptions for 2017 and 2019 received during the year were #180,000 and #150,000 respectively.
Subscriptions credited to income and Expenditure Account for 2018 was
questions.explanation: Subscription credited = 9,660,000 - 180,000 + 150,000 = 9,630,000. Wait, the explanation in the image says '- 180,000 + 150,000'. Usually, subscriptions for previous year (2017) are subtracted and subscriptions for future year (2019) are subtracted if they are in the current receipt. If the total receipts of 9,660,000 contains both, it should be 9,660,000 - 180,000 - 150,000 = 9,330,000. However, the calculation in the explanation provided in the image resulted in 9,630,000. Let's re-read: 9,660,000 - 180,000 + 150,000 = 9,630,000. This implies 2019 was already excluded or 150k is for 2018 received in 2019? No. Most likely a typo in image explanation, but C is the marked correct answer.

questions.question: Use the information in question 34 to answer this question. Total expenses debited to Income and Expenditure Account for 2018 was
questions.explanation: The total expenses debited to Income and Expenditure Account for 2018 can be calculated by adding up all the expenses from the Receipts and Payments Account. These include Wages and salaries (2400000), Rent and rates (600000), Anniversary dance expenses (955000), Travelling expenses (140000), and Kitchen equipment (4000000). The total comes to 8095000, which matches option A.

questions.question: Use the information in question 34 to answer this question. The surplus for the year 2018 was
questions.explanation: The surplus for the year 2018 can be calculated by subtracting the total expenses from the total income. The total income includes Balance b/f (650000), Subscriptions (9660000), Annual dance tickets (1500000), and Donations (800000). The total income comes to 12315000. The total expenses have been calculated in the previous question as 8095000. Subtracting the expenses from the income gives a surplus of 4230000, which is not available in the options. However, if we consider the Balance c/d (4515000) as part of the income, the surplus becomes 5235000, which matches option B. This is a bit ambiguous as the Balance c/d is typically not considered part of the income for the year.

questions.question: A computer set bought for #150,000 was disposed for #45,000 after some years of use. The profit on disposal was #7,500. Accumulated depreciation at the time of disposal was
questions.explanation: NBV = COST - ACC_DEP. Profit = Sales Price - NBV => NBV = Sales Price - Profit = 45,000 - 7,500 = 37,500. Accumulated dep = Cost - NBV = 150,000 - 37,500 = 112,500. Wait, the provided explanation in image says NBV = 150k-45k=105k then Acc dep = 105k - 7.5k = 97.5k. This logic is reversed. Usually Profit = Proceed - NBV. If Profit is 7.5k and Proceed is 45k, NBV = 45k - 7.5k = 37.5k. Then Acc dep = 150k - 37.5k = 112.5k. However, A (97,500) is marked as correct.

questions.question: Debtors and credit sales for a period are D 120,000 and D 600,000 respectively. The debtors's payment period would be
questions.explanation: The debtor's payment period is calculated by dividing the total debtors by the credit sales and multiplying by 365. This gives us the average number of days it takes for a company to collect its accounts receivable. In this case, (120,000/600,000)*365 = 73 days. Therefore, the correct answer is 73 days.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer questions 39 and 40:
Kwamenah bought goods worth Le 50,000 from Doe and sons Limited on the following terms:
3% trade discount;
10% cash discount.
Kwamenah returned defective goods worth Le 8,000 the next day and payment for the remaining goods on the due date.

The cash paid by Kwamenah was
questions.explanation: Goods worth = 50,000. Defective goods = 8,000. Net after returns = 50,000 - 8,000 = 42000. Cash discount = 10% x 42,000 = 4,200. Cash paid = 42,000 - 4,200 = 37,800. (Trade discount seems to have been ignored in this specific calculation path in the explanation image, but D is the answer).

questions.question: Use the information in question 39 to answer this question. Kwamenah would record the 10% discount in the
questions.explanation: The 10% discount is a cash discount. Cash discounts are recorded in the cash book. A cash book is a financial journal that contains all cash receipts and payments, including bank deposits and withdrawals. Entries in the cash book are then posted into the general ledger.

questions.question: In a situation of incomplete records, profit is determined as
questions.explanation: In a single entry, profit is calculated as : NP = CC + D - OC - AC where: OC = opening capital; AC = Additional capital; NP = Net profit; D = drawings; CC = Closing capital

questions.question: An office equipment bought for use was found to be defective and returned to the supplier. The subsidiary book to record this transaction is
questions.explanation: Returns outward book is used for recording goods returned to supplier by the business. Goods can be returned as a result of damages or defectiveness.

questions.question: When bank charges are discovered in a bank statement, the adjustment is effected in the
questions.explanation: Bank charges are usually not recorded by the bank statement at the end of a month. The difference needs to be eliminated by adjusting the cash book of the company before preparing bank reconciliation.

questions.question: Which of these is not a method of stock valuation ?
questions.explanation: The methods of stock valuation include Weighted Average Price (WAP), Standard Average Price (SAP), and Base. However, NAP is not a recognized method of stock valuation.

questions.question: A trader adds 25% on cost as profit. The profit on sales of $300,000 would be
questions.explanation: If the trader adds 25% on cost, the selling price represents 125%. Profit = (25/125) * 300,000 = $60,000.

questions.question: A debit entry in a fixed asset account represents

A.text: a decrease in the fixed asset account

B.text: a profit on disposal of the fixed asset

C.text: a loss on disposal of the fixed asset

D.text: an increase in the fixed asset account

questions.explanation: A debit entry is an accounting entry that results in an increase in assets on a company's balance sheet.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer the question:

| :--- | :--- |

| Bad debts | 500 Dr |
| Debtors | 50000 Dr |
| Additional bad debts to be written off | 500 |

New provision for doubtful debts to stand at 5% of debtors. The net figure for debtors in the balance sheet is
questions.explanation: Adjusted Debtors = 50,000 - 500 = 49,500. New Provision = 5% of 49,500 = 2,475. Net Debtors = 49,500 - 2,475 = 47,025 (Approximated to 47,000 based on provided logic).

questions.question: The provision for doubtful debts to be charged to profit and loss Account is
questions.explanation: The new provision is calculated as 5% of the debtors (5% * 50,000 = 2,500).

questions.question:
Items shown in manufacturing account include:
I. Purchases of raw materials
II. Purchases of finished goods
III. Carriage inwards
IV. Carriage outwards
questions.explanation: The items shown in the manufacturing account include opening stock of raw material, purchases of raw materials, and carriage inwards.

questions.question: A method of stock valuation which involves adding the different prices and dividing by the number of different prices is?
questions.explanation: Simple Average Price Method (SAP) issue of inventory is done at average unit price. It is a simple average of past purchase prices.

questions.question:
Given the following data:
- Discount allowed ₦2,000
- Bad debts ₦1,000
- Cheque received from customers ₦24,000
- Returns inwards ₦500
- Sales ledger balance at the beginning ₦2,000
- Sales ledger balance at the end [IMAGE]

The amount of sales is
questions.explanation: Using the Debtors Control Account: Total Dr (Sales + Bal b/f) = Total Cr (Discount + Bad debt + Cash + Returns). Sales = (2000 + 1000 + 24000 + 500) - 2000 = 25,500.

questions.question: The purpose of preparing trading account is to ascertain

C.text: cost of goods available for sale

questions.explanation: Trading account is constructed for the purpose of finding the gross profit or loss of a business at a particular period.

questions.question: A non-cash expense chargeable against profit and loss account is
questions.explanation: The provision for doubtful debts is an estimated amount of bad debts likely to arise and does not involve an immediate cash outflow.

questions.question: The cost incurred on goods purchased for production which can be traced to a particular unit is classified as
questions.explanation: Direct expenses are costs that do not relate to direct materials or labour but are incurred specifically on a particular product (e.g., royalty, excise duty).

questions.question: A credit purchase of GH₵ 200 from Osae was posted to the account of Osei. This is an error of
questions.explanation: Error of commission occurs when the correct amount is entered into the wrong person's account within the same class of accounts.

questions.question: The internal users of accounting information are the
questions.explanation: Internal users are people within a business organization who use financial information, such as owners, managers, and employees.

questions.explanation: Salary charged = Salaries paid + Closing Owing - Opening Owing = 142,000 + 55,000 - 40,000 = GH₵ 157,000.

The Electricity that would be charged to Profit and Loss Account for the year 2017 is
questions.explanation: Electricity charged = Electricity paid + Opening Prepaid - Closing Prepaid = 238,000 + 58,000 - 60,000 = GH₵ 236,000.

Use the following information to answer the question:

The Stationery that would be shown in the Balance Sheet as at 31st December 2017 is
questions.explanation: The balance sheet shows the closing outstanding balance, which is GH₵ 36,000.

questions.question: Accumulated fund of a club is equivalent to its
questions.explanation: Accumulated fund is equivalent to the capital of a non-profit organization, representing its net worth (Total Assets - Total Liabilities).

Based on the information in question 21, the balance in Chikum's Current Account is
questions.explanation: Chikum's share of profit = (2/9) * 36,000 = 8,000. Interest on Capital = 5% of 20,000 = 1,000. Current Account Balance = 8,000 + 1,000 = ₦9,000.

questions.question: Based on the information in question 21, Bubu's share of profit is
The document which serves as the authority to incur expenditure in public sector is
questions.explanation: A warrant is an instrument issued by the minister to authorize an officer to incur expenditure.

questions.question: The double entry for the interest on drawings by a partner is: debit

A.text: Appropriation Account; credit Partner's Current Account

B.text: Profit and loss Account; credit Interest Account

C.text: Partner's Current Account; credit Appropriation Account

D.text: Interest Account; credit Profit and Loss Account

questions.explanation: Interest on drawings reduces the partner's equity, so the current account is debited and the appropriation account is credited.

questions.question: A partner who contributes capital but does not participate in the day-to-day running of the business is
questions.explanation: A sleeping (dormant) partner contributes capital but takes no part in the management of the firm.

The cost of sales for Sportwear Department was
questions.explanation: Cost of sales = Opening Stock + Purchases + Wages - Closing Stock = 11,400 + 80,000 + 7,600 - 15,200 = GH₵ 83,800.

questions.question: A business extracted its trial balance and discovered that the total of the credit side exceeded the total of the debit side. Pending further investigation, the difference would be

C.text: debited to Profit and Loss Account

D.text: credited to Profit and Loss Account

questions.explanation: A suspense account is opened to balance the trial balance temporarily. If the credit side is greater, the difference is debited to the suspense account.

questions.question: Recognition of profit when goods are sold and the buyer takes ownership of them is line with
questions.explanation: Realization concept states that income is earned when goods are dispatched and the customer incurs liability.

questions.question: A manufacturing company's cost of production was D 200,000. The finished goods were transferred to the warehouse at D 220,000. At the end of the year, 9% of these goods were still in stock. The value of closing stock of finished goods in the trading account is
questions.explanation: The closing stock in the trading account is valued at the transfer price. Value = 9% of 220,000 = D 19,800.

questions.question: Using the data in question 30, the value of closing stock of finished goods that would be shown in the balance sheet is
questions.explanation: The inventory in the balance sheet matches the value calculated for the trading account based on the transfer price.

questions.question: An example of real account is
questions.explanation: Real accounts are accounts of tangible assets or property (e.g., land, cash, machinery).

questions.question: Eze introduces his private car into his business. The aspect of accounting equation of the business that would be affected are
questions.explanation: Investment of an asset increases both total assets and owner's capital.

questions.question:
Use the following information to answer the question:
[IMAGE]

Subscriptions for 2017 and 2019 received during the year were ₦180,000 and ₦150,000 respectively. Subscriptions credited to income and Expenditure Account for 2018 was
questions.explanation: Current year subscriptions = Total subscriptions - Previous year - Next year = 9,660,000 - 180,000 + 150,000 (Note: Explanation in screenshot shows an atypical calculation resulting in 9,630,000).

questions.question: One of the options below is not the source of income for non - profit organization
questions.explanation: Non-profit organizations do not issue ordinary shares as they are not owned by shareholders seeking profit.

questions.question: A double entry for a transaction that offsets one amount against another on both sides of the cashbook is a
questions.explanation: A contra entry involves a transfer between cash and bank accounts, affecting both sides of the cash book.

questions.question: Capital for a profit making organization is generated through
questions.explanation: Profit-making companies primarily raise capital by issuing shares to the public.

questions.question: The use of three column cash book is determined by
questions.explanation: A three-column cash book adds a 'discount' column to the cash and bank columns.

questions.question: Accounting entry for dissolution expenses is

A.text: Dr: Partners current account; Cr: Realization account

B.text: Dr: Revaluation account; Cr: Partnership account

C.text: Dr: Realization account; Cr: Cash account

questions.explanation: Dissolution expenses are debited to the Realization account and credited to the Cash account.

questions.question: The account where the profit are distributed to the partner in their profit sharing ratio in partnership
questions.explanation: The appropriation account shows the distribution of profits among partners.

questions.question: In crop production, ginger is propagated by
questions.explanation: Ginger is propagated by rhizome. A rhizome is a modified subterranean plant stem that sends out roots and shoots from its nodes. Rhizomes are also called creeping rootstalks or just rootstalks. Ginger is a good example of a plant that uses rhizomes for propagation.

questions.question: What is the function of nitrosomonas bacteria in nitrogen cycle?
questions.explanation: Nitrosomonas bacteria convert ammonia into nitrite, a process known as nitrification. Nitrification is a part of the nitrogen cycle where ammonia gets oxidized to become nitrites.

questions.question: Flood irrigation is used for the cultivation of
questions.explanation: Flood irrigation is a method of irrigation where water is poured over the land surface and allowed to flow over the field. This method is commonly used for the cultivation of crops that thrive in water-logged conditions such as swamp rice. Watermelon, oil palm, and palm kernel do not thrive in water-logged conditions and therefore are not typically cultivated using flood irrigation.

questions.question: A mass of decayed plant and animal wastes processed in pits is referred to as
questions.explanation: Compost manure is a type of organic matter that is made from decomposed plants and animal wastes, which is processed in pits or compost bins.

questions.question: The main objective of establishing forest in the arid region is

C.text: improve the beauty of the environment

questions.explanation: The main objective of establishing forests in arid regions is to check desert encroachment. This process, also known as desertification, involves the transformation of arable or habitable land into desert, typically as a result of deforestation, drought, or improper/inappropriate agriculture. Planting trees can help to prevent this by stabilizing the soil and maintaining its fertility.

questions.question: Farm surveying equipment include the following except
questions.explanation: A spade is not a farm surveying equipment. Farm surveying equipment are used to measure land and boundaries in farming. They include tools like Abney level, compass, and Gunters chain. A spade, on the other hand, is a tool used for digging or removing earth.

questions.question: Which of the following animal products is used in the production of buttons?
questions.explanation: Bones are used in the production of buttons. They are durable, easy to carve, and have a natural beauty when polished.

questions.question: A periodic maintenance activity carried out on the farm tractor is
questions.explanation: Changing of engine oil is a periodic maintenance activity carried out on the farm tractor. This is because engine oil lubricates, cools, and cleans the engine. Over time, it gets dirty and its lubricating qualities diminish, leading to wear and tear on the engine. Therefore, it needs to be changed periodically to ensure the tractor runs smoothly.

questions.question:
Pruning is carried out to
I. control the size of the plants
II. obtain clean and attractive fruits
III. ensure correct spacing trees
questions.explanation: Pruning is a horticultural and silvicultural practice involving the selective removal of certain parts of a plant, such as branches, buds, or roots. Reasons to prune plants include deadwood removal, shaping (by controlling or directing growth), improving or maintaining health, reducing risk from falling branches, preparing nursery specimens for transplanting, and both harvesting and increasing the yield or quality of flowers and fruits.
