questions.question: Which of the following processes will not introduce carbondioxide into the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a process used by plants and other organisms to convert light energy, usually from the Sun, into chemical energy that can be later released to fuel the organisms' activities. This chemical energy is stored in carbohydrate molecules, such as sugars, which are synthesized from carbon dioxide and water. Hence, photosynthesis does not introduce carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, instead it uses carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

questions.question:
The diagram above illustrates a part of the mammalian skeleton. use it to answer this question.
The function of the part labelled I is to [IMAGE]

B.text: provide surface for attachment of the muscle

C.text: provide support to the spinal cord

questions.explanation: The part labelled 1 is the Cervical Vertebral (Neural) Canal. The bony cervical vertebral canal serves as a protective conduit for the spinal cord and as an exit point of the cervical nerve roots. Because of the bulging of the cervical neuromeres as well as the other fibers that must traverse the cervical vertebral canal to reach the lower portions of the body, the cervical spinal cord occupies a significantly greater proportion of the space available in the spinal canal relative to the space occupied by the thoracic and lumbar spinal cord.

questions.question:
I. Cat
II. Frog
III. bird
IV. Fish

Oviparity is the type of fertilization exhibited by the organism labelled?
questions.explanation: Oviparity is a mode of reproduction in animals in which embryos develop inside eggs that are laid externally. This is common among birds, reptiles, and some fishes. Among the options given, birds (III) exhibit oviparity.

questions.question: What is the difference between viviparous and oviparous animals?
questions.explanation: The difference between viviparous and oviparous animals is the method of development of the young ones. Viviparous animals give birth to live young ones while oviparous animals lay eggs that develop and hatch outside the mother's body. The possession of yolked eggs is a characteristic of oviparous animals.

questions.question: The organ located in the mammalian abdominal cavity just below the diaphragm and lying on top of the stomach is the
questions.explanation: The organ located in the mammalian abdominal cavity just below the diaphragm and lying on top of the stomach is the liver. The liver is a large, meaty organ that sits on the right side of the belly. It is protected by the rib cage. The liver has two large sections, called the right and the left lobes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best explains the term aestivation?

A.text: A method of survival adopted by plants during dry season

B.text: A method of survival adopted by animals under favourable condition

C.text: Movement of animals over long distance

D.text: a device for survival during a period of extreme food and water shortage

questions.explanation: Aestivation is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation, characterized by inactivity and a lowered metabolic rate, that is entered in response to high temperatures and arid conditions. It is a survival strategy used by many invertebrates, like snails and crustaceans, and by some mammals, like the North American desert tortoise, to survive the hottest and driest part of the year. It is a device for survival during a period of extreme food and water shortage.

questions.question:
Below is a List of the levels of organization in organisms.  
i. Tissue  
ii. System  
iii. Cell  
iv. Organ  

The correct sequence of the levels in an increasing order of complexity is?
questions.explanation: The correct sequence of the levels of organization in organisms in increasing order of complexity is Cell, Tissue, Organ, System. This is because cells combine to form tissues, tissues combine to form organs, and organs work together in systems. Therefore, the correct answer is 'III, I, IV, II'.

questions.question: Water rises most rapidly in
questions.explanation: Water rises most rapidly in sandy soil. This is due to the large pore spaces between the sand particles which allow water to move through the soil quickly. Clayey soil, sandy-loam soil, and loamy soil have smaller pore spaces and therefore water moves through them more slowly. Therefore, the correct answer is 'sandy soil'.

questions.question: A difference between the auricle and the ventricle of the mammalian heart is that the

C.text: walls of the ventricles are more muscular than that of the auricles

questions.explanation: The walls of the ventricles are more muscular than that of the auricles. This is because the ventricles have to pump blood out of the heart to the rest of the body, which requires more force and therefore more muscle.

questions.question: If the petals of a flowering plant are removed, which of the following processes is likely to be affected?
questions.explanation: If the petals of a flowering plant are removed, pollination is likely to be affected. Petals are often brightly colored or unusually shaped to attract pollinators. Without petals, pollinators may not be attracted to the flower, thus affecting the process of pollination.

questions.question: In testing for starch in a leaf, the function of alcohol is to

A.text: soften the veins of the leaf

B.text: kill the cells of the leaf

C.text: extract the chlorophyll from the leaf

D.text: aid iodine to penetrate the cells

questions.explanation: In testing for starch in a leaf, the function of alcohol is to extract the chlorophyll from the leaf.

questions.question: Which of the following can be used for vegetative propagation?
questions.explanation: Vegetative propagation involves the use of parts of plant in multiplying the plant or when a new plant grows out of a parent plant, without the use of seeds. Ginger stem (rhizomes) can be used for vegetative propagation.

questions.question: In a cross involving a heterozygous red flowered plant (Rr) and a white flowered plant (rr). What is the probability that the offspring will be Rr?
questions.explanation: In a cross between a heterozygous red flowered plant (Rr) and a white flowered plant (rr), the possible genotypes of the offspring are Rr and rr. The probability of getting Rr is 1/2. This is because the heterozygous red flowered plant (Rr) can pass on either R or r to the offspring, and the white flowered plant (rr) can only pass on r. Therefore, the combinations are Rr and rr, making the probability of Rr 1/2.

questions.question: The enzyme that acts on milk in the stomach is
questions.explanation: The enzyme that acts on milk in the stomach is Renin. Renin is an enzyme that is produced by cells in the stomach and it helps in the digestion of milk by curdling it. This process breaks down the protein in milk into smaller, more digestible pieces.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an organ?
questions.explanation: An organ is a group of tissues that perform a specific function or group of functions. Hair is not an organ, it is a structure made of keratin, a type of protein.

questions.question: One major difference between osmosis and diffusion is that diffusion

A.text: does not need a semi-permeable membrane

B.text: takes place only in a liquid medium

C.text: does not take place in living tissues

D.text: takes place only in a gaseous medium

questions.explanation: It should be noted that while osmosis needs a semi-permeable membrane, diffusion does not require a semi-permeable membrane.

questions.question: Which of the following examples best describes camouflage coloration in organism?

A.text: A zebra with vertical stripes on the body

B.text: Butterfly which has a red coloration on the wing

C.text: A green snake in a green grass

D.text: Certain tree frogs with bright color pattern

questions.explanation: Camouflage coloration is a type of coloration or patterns which allow an organism to blend in with its environment. This helps the organism to hide from predators or prey. A green snake in green grass is a perfect example of this as the snake can easily blend in with the grass and remain undetected.

questions.question: In which part of the kidney does ultra-filtration take place?
questions.explanation: Ultra-filtration in the kidney takes place in the Bowman's capsule. This is the initial stage of urine formation where blood is filtered under high pressure, and waste products are removed.

questions.question: In a plant of genotype Tt, what is the probability that a gamete will contain gene t?
questions.explanation: In a plant of genotype Tt, the probability that a gamete will contain gene t is 1/2. This is because the plant has two alleles for the trait, T and t. When gametes are formed, each gamete gets one of the two alleles. Therefore, the probability that a gamete will get the t allele is 1/2.

questions.question: The stomata of a leaf performs the same function as
questions.explanation: The stomata of a leaf perform the same function as the spiracles of insects. Both are involved in the process of gas exchange. Stomata are tiny openings or pores in plant tissue that allow for gas exchange. Spiracles are small openings on the surface of some animals, which usually lead to respiratory systems. They are used for breathing, similar to how stomata are used in plants.

questions.question: Which of the following is the difference between mitosis and meiosis?

C.text: Alignment of chromosomes along the equatorial plane

D.text: Replication of the chromosomes and the cell organelles

questions.explanation: In mitosis, there is no pairing of homologous chromosomes; while in meiosis, there is pairing of homologous chromosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following does not happen during inspiration?
questions.explanation: During inspiration (inhalation), the diaphragm contracts, not relaxes. This contraction moves the diaphragm down, increasing the volume of the thoracic cavity, and thus decreasing the pressure inside the chest, allowing air to flow into the lungs.

questions.question: Which of the following make up the bee caste?
questions.explanation: The colony of bees consists of three (3) castes - queen (reproductive females), workers (sterile females) and drones (males).

questions.question: The mode of nutrition which describes feeding habit in animals is
questions.explanation: The feeding habit in animals is holozoic. NOTE: Holozoic nutrition is a type of nutrition in which food is obtained as a solid organic material, eaten, digested and absorbed into the body

questions.question:
Use the diagram above to answer this question
The part labelled II in the diagram is the [IMAGE]
questions.explanation: The part labelled II is the endosperm. The endosperm is a nutrient-rich tissue that surrounds and nourishes the embryo in the seeds of many flowering plants. It is formed from the fusion of the female gametophyte's two polar nuclei with one of the pollen grain's two sperm nuclei. This triple fusion event is unique to flowering plants and is one of the defining characteristics of their seeds.

questions.question:
Use the diagram above to answer this question
The part labelled IV in the diagram is the [IMAGE]
questions.explanation: The part labelled IV is the radicle. The radicle is the embryonic root of a plant. It is the first part of the embryo to emerge from the seed during germination. The radicle grows downward into the soil, anchoring the seedling. In gymnosperms and dicotyledons (angiosperms with two seed leaves), the radicle becomes a taproot.

questions.question:
Use the diagram above to answer this question
The function of the part labelled V in the diagram is the [IMAGE]
questions.explanation: The part labelled V is the coleorhiza which protects the radicle. The coleorhiza is a protective sheath that surrounds the radicle (embryonic root) of monocotyledonous plants (plants with one seed leaf). It develops from the embryonic root cap and is made up of a layer of cells that are tough and durable.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of the blood?
questions.explanation: The blood has many functions in the body including maintenance of body temperature, formation of clot to prevent excessive bleeding and transportation of excretory materials. However, the distribution of bile is not a function of the blood. Bile is produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. It is then released into the small intestine to aid in the digestion of fats.

questions.question: Termites are called social insects because

A.text: the king and queen do mate

B.text: the queen allocates duties to the workers

D.text: the nests are built by the nasute soldiers

questions.explanation: Termites are called social insects because they live together in communities. This is a characteristic of social insects, where individuals of the same species live in an organized way, often dividing labor and cooperating in tasks such as foraging and defense.

questions.question: The role of bacteria in the nitrogen cycle includes the following except
questions.explanation: Bacteria play several roles in the nitrogen cycle, including fixing atmospheric nitrogen, converting ammonia to nitrites, and converting nitrites to nitrates. However, they do not convert carbohydrates to carbon dioxide. This process is typically associated with respiration in animals and fermentation in yeast, not with bacteria in the nitrogen cycle.

questions.question: How many chromosomes will be present in a gamete if the somatic cell has eight (8) chromosomes?
questions.explanation: In a sexually reproducing organism, the number of chromosomes in a gamete is half the number of chromosomes in a somatic cell. This is because gametes are produced by a process called meiosis, which reduces the number of chromosomes by half. Therefore, if a somatic cell has 8 chromosomes, a gamete will have 4 chromosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms has homodont dentition?
questions.explanation: Lizard (a reptile) has homodont dentition. Homodont dentition - Pisces, Amphibian, Reptile, Amphibian. Heterodont Dentition - Mammal {Rabbit (A), Man (B), Sheep (C), Cat (D)}

questions.question: The hormone that promotes secondary sexual characteristics in females is
questions.explanation: Oestrogen promotes secondary sexual characteristics in females. Insulin regulates blood glucose level. Oxytocin is responsible for milk let-down during breast feeding. Testosterone promotes sperm formation and sex sexual characteristics in males. Thyroxin accelerates metabolism rate and controls growth.

questions.question:
The diagram below is the root system of a leguminous plant. Study and use it to answer this question.

Structures labeled II are [IMAGE]
questions.explanation: The structure labelled II are the root nodules. Root nodules are small, round bumps that form on the roots of leguminous plants, such as beans, peas, and lentils. They are formed by a symbiotic relationship between the plant and a type of bacteria called rhizobia.

questions.question: The structures labelled II are associated with [IMAGE]
questions.explanation: The structure labelled II are associated with nitrogen-fixing bacteria. These bacteria reside in the part labelled II fixing atmospheric nitrogen to the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following organs is concerned with detoxification of harmful compounds?
questions.explanation: The liver is the organ primarily responsible for the detoxification of harmful compounds in the body. It does this by metabolizing drugs and toxins into more soluble compounds that can be easily excreted by the kidneys.

questions.question: Which of the following processes removes \( CO_2 \) from the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis takes \( CO_2 \) from the atmosphere. This \( CO_2 \) combines with water in the presence of sunlight energy (trapped by chlorophyll) to form glucose and oxygen. \(6CO_2 + 6H_2O \rightarrow C_6H_{12}O_6 + 6O_2\) (in the presence of sunlight & chlorophyll)

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the circulation of blood is not correct?

A.text: Blood is pumped out of the heart through the aorta

B.text: Oxygenated blood is carried to the left auricle from the lungs

C.text: The oxygenated blood enters the lungs through the pulmonary vein

D.text: Deoxygenated blood flow into the heart through the Vena cava

questions.explanation: Oxygenated blood does not enter the lungs through the pulmonary vein rather it goes out of the heart into the lungs through the pulmonary vein.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms lacks a reproductive organ?
questions.explanation: Chlamydomonas is a genus of green algae that lacks a reproductive organ. It reproduces asexually through a process called binary fission, where the cell divides into two equal parts, each becoming a new organism. In contrast, Toad, Pride of Barbados (a type of flowering plant), and Cockroach all have reproductive organs and reproduce sexually.

questions.question: The collective name for the female parts of the flower is
questions.explanation: The collective name for the female part of a flower is gynoecium. It consists of the ovary, style and stigma.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms exhibits both plant and animal characteristics?
questions.explanation: Euglena is an organism which exhibits both plant and animal characteristics. The animal characteristics of Euglena include: - The presence of pellicle for flexibility - The presence of flagella for movement - The presence of eye spot for photosensitivity. The plant characteristics of Euglena include: - The presence of chloroplast for photosynthesis - The presence of paralympic granules for starch storage.

questions.question: Which of these is not an excretory product of plant?
questions.explanation: Sweat is not an excretory product of plants. It is a product of the sweat glands in animals, particularly humans, and is used for thermoregulation. Plants do not have sweat glands and do not produce sweat. They do, however, excrete other substances such as tannins, gums, and alkaloids.

questions.question: Oil is sprayed over stagnant water in which mosquitoes breed in order to

A.text: lubricate the larva for easy movement

B.text: provide landing surface for the adult

D.text: prevent larva or pupa from attaching itself to the surface

questions.explanation: Oil is sprayed over stagnant water in which mosquitoes breed in order to reduce the surface tension of water. This prevents the larva or pupa from attacking itself to the water surface.

questions.question: Which of these is not a function of the kidney?

C.text: maintenance of acid-base balance in the body

questions.explanation: The kidney is responsible for the production of urine, maintenance of acid-base balance in the body, and osmo-regulation. However, the production of bile is not a function of the kidney but of the liver. Bile is a digestive fluid that helps in the digestion and absorption of fats.

questions.question: Which of the following features acts as a shock absorber to the developing embryo?
questions.explanation: The amniotic fluid acts as a shock absorber to the developing embryo. It is a clear, yellowish liquid that surrounds the unborn baby (fetus) during pregnancy. It is contained in the amniotic sac. It protects the fetus from physical shocks, provides it with fluids that it can breathe and swallow, and helps with the development of the fetus's lungs, digestive system, muscles, and bones.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms respires through the body surface?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular organism which respires by simple diffusion through its body surface (cell membrane).

questions.question: Which of the following is a method of artificial vegetative propagation?
questions.explanation: Artificial vegetative propagation is the use of part of the parent plant to multiply the plant. The methods of artificial vegetative propagation include: - Budding - Grafting - Layering - Cutting - Marcotting

questions.question: Which of the following plants is a carnivore?
questions.explanation: Sundew is a carnivorous plant. It is one of the few green plants that supplement their nutrients with organic food, by trapping insects and other animals. Often, they do this to obtain NITROGEN.

questions.question: Blood is made of ___ major components
questions.explanation: Blood is made up of four major components: red blood cells (erythrocytes), white blood cells (leukocytes), platelets (thrombocytes), and plasma. Red blood cells carry oxygen, white blood cells fight infections, platelets help with clotting, and plasma is the liquid component that carries cells and platelets.

questions.question: Vacuoles are found

D.text: both in plant and animal cell

questions.explanation: Vacuoles are a type of organelle found in both plant and animal cells. They are involved in a variety of tasks, including nutrient storage, waste management, predator deterrence, sexual reproduction, physical support, and growth. However, they are more prominent in plant cells.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an example of social insect?
questions.explanation: Social insects are those that live in colonies or have a division of labor, such as bees, termites, and wasps. Mealybugs, on the other hand, do not exhibit these social behaviors and are therefore not considered social insects.

questions.question: A reproductive communication between males and females of a species that ends in sexual union is called
questions.explanation: The reproductive communication between males and females of a species that ends in sexual union is called courtship. Courtship is a period during which a couple develop a romantic relationship, especially with a view to marriage. It involves a series of activities by which two people show romantic interest in each other and usually leads to mating. The other options, dating, introduction, and engagement, are stages in human relationships but do not necessarily lead to sexual union.

questions.question: Which of the organisms does not possess streamlined body for easy movement in water
questions.explanation: Crabs do not have a streamlined body for easy movement in water. They are not primarily swimmers but rather walk on the seafloor.

questions.question: Insects and mollusks both have
questions.explanation: Insects and mollusks both have an open circulatory system. In an open circulatory system, the blood (or hemolymph in the case of insects and mollusks) is not enclosed in blood vessels but is pumped into a cavity called a hemocoel and comes directly into contact with tissues.

questions.question: An individual phenotype is the
questions.explanation: An individual's phenotype is the observable traits of an individual. An individual's phenotype is the combination of their observable characteristics or traits

questions.question: Population study in ecology involves the following steps except

A.text: Select the sampling method to be used

D.text: Collect, count and record the different types of organisms present.

questions.explanation: The steps involved in population study in ecology include choosing the habitat, selecting the sampling method to be used, and collecting, counting, and recording the different types of organisms present. However, ignoring the dominant species is not a step involved in population study in ecology.

questions.question: Photosynthetic Nutrition is also known as
questions.explanation: Photosynthetic nutrition is also known as holophytic nutrition. This is a type of autotrophic nutrition where an organism produces organic substances from inorganic ones using light energy. Holozoic, carnivorous, and parasitic nutrition are types of heterotrophic nutrition, where organisms obtain organic substances from other organisms.

questions.question: Based on their food types, animals are grouped into following except
questions.explanation: Animals are grouped into different categories based on their food types. These categories include Carnivores (meat-eaters), Herbivores (plant-eaters), and Omnivores (both meat and plant eaters). However, 'Stomivores' is not a recognized category in this classification.

questions.question: The local biomes particular to Nigeria include the following except
questions.explanation: Nigeria, being a tropical country, does not have temperate shrubland. The biomes particular to Nigeria include mangrove swamps, tropical rain forests, and savannas. Temperate shrubland is found in regions with a Mediterranean climate which is not applicable to Nigeria.

questions.question: The following are characteristics of kingdom Animalia except
questions.explanation: One of the key characteristics of kingdom Animalia is that their cells do not have cell walls. This is a feature that distinguishes them from plants and fungi. Therefore, the statement 'Cells have cell walls' is not a characteristic of kingdom Animalia. The other options are correct: animals are heterotrophic (they obtain nutrients by consuming other organisms), and many, but not all, animals have nervous systems.

questions.question: ______ is a directional movement or response of a whole organism from one place to another in response to external stimuli such as light
questions.explanation: Taxis or tactic movement is a directional movement or response of a whole organism from one place to another in response to external stimuli such as light. This is different from tropism, which is a growth movement whose direction is determined by the direction of the stimulus, and nastic movements, which are non-directional responses to stimuli.

questions.question: The theory of use and disuse was proposed by
questions.explanation: The theory of use and disuse was proposed by Jean-Baptiste Lamarck. This theory, also known as Lamarckism, suggests that an organism can change during its lifetime in response to its environment, and those changes are passed on to its offspring. It is one of the early theories of evolution predating Darwin's theory of natural selection.

questions.question: ______ is defined as the ability of an organism to live successfully in a particular habitat as a result of its structure, appearance and behaviour
questions.explanation: Adaptation is the ability of an organism to live successfully in a particular habitat as a result of its structure, appearance, and behaviour. This process takes place over many generations and is a result of evolution.

questions.question: Giraffe's long neck is used to describe the theory of
questions.explanation: The long neck of a giraffe is often used to describe the theory of natural selection. This theory, proposed by Charles Darwin, suggests that traits that enhance survival and reproduction will be passed on to future generations. Giraffes with longer necks could reach more food and survive better, so they passed the long-neck trait to their offspring.

questions.question: Malaria is caused by which of the following vectors?
questions.explanation: Malaria is caused by the Plasmodium parasite, which is transmitted to humans through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes. Therefore, Anopheles mosquitoes are the correct vector for malaria.

questions.question: Protozoa have contractile vacuole for
questions.explanation: Protozoa have contractile vacuoles for osmoregulation in water. Osmoregulation is the process by which an organism regulates its internal water balance. In the case of protozoa, the contractile vacuole collects and pumps excess water out of the cell, thereby preventing the cell from bursting due to osmotic pressure.

questions.question: Population characteristics includes all the following except
questions.explanation: Population characteristics include population size, population density, and population frequency. However, population maturity is not a characteristic of a population.

questions.question: One of the following is an example of discontinuous variation
questions.explanation: Discontinuous variation refers to the variation in characteristics that have a limited number of variations, such as blood group or eye color. In this case, left-handedness is an example of discontinuous variation because a person can either be left-handed or right-handed. There is no intermediate between the two. Body weight, body height, and skin color are examples of continuous variation because they can take on a range of values within a certain limit.

questions.question: The instrument used for measuring the direction of the wind is
questions.explanation: A wind vane is an instrument used to measure the direction of the wind. It is also known as a weather vane or weathercock. It is typically used as an architectural ornament to the highest point of a building. The wind vane shows the direction from which the wind is coming and is usually combined with an anemometer to measure wind speed.

questions.question: ______ is the process by which a fairly constant internal environment is maintained in an organism
questions.explanation: Homeostasis is the process by which a fairly constant internal environment is maintained in an organism. It involves the regulation of the internal conditions of a body to maintain a constant state, for example, the human body regulates temperature, blood sugar level, water content, etc. to maintain a stable internal environment.

questions.question: Human beings have ___ sets of teeth in their lifetime
questions.explanation: Human beings have two sets of teeth in their lifetime. The first set is the deciduous or 'baby' teeth, which eventually fall out and are replaced by the permanent or 'adult' teeth. The process of tooth replacement is known as exfoliation.

questions.question: ______ is an association between two organisms living together in which only one benefits from the association while the other neither benefits nor is harmed
questions.explanation: Commensalism is an association between two organisms where one benefits and the other is not significantly affected. It is a type of symbiotic relationship between organisms, where one organism benefits from the other without affecting it. This is different from parasitism (where one organism benefits at the expense of the other) and predation (where one organism kills and consumes the other).

questions.question: which of the following is not an organ of vegetative propagation?
questions.explanation: Vegetative propagation is a form of asexual reproduction in plants. It involves multiplication from vegetative parts like roots, stems, and leaves. Bulb, runners, and suckers are all organs of vegetative propagation. However, gametes are reproductive cells (sex cells) involved in sexual reproduction, not vegetative propagation. Therefore, the correct answer is 'gametes'.

questions.question: The following are functions of rough endoplasmic reticulum except

A.text: They help in formation of enzymes and protein

B.text: They destroy worn out part of the cell

C.text: They transport metabolic products within cytoplasm between the cytoplasm and nucleus

D.text: They inter connect the organelles of the cell

questions.explanation: The destruction of worn-out parts of the cell is not a function of the rough endoplasmic reticulum. This function is typically performed by lysosomes, which are organelles that contain enzymes that break down waste materials and cellular debris.

questions.question: Plants such as seaweeds are naturally found in marine habitat with one of the this adaptive feature
questions.explanation: Seaweeds, being marine algae, have a structure called holdfast which helps them to attach to the rocky seafloor. This is an adaptive feature that allows them to stay in place in the often turbulent ocean environment. The holdfast is not a true root system, but serves a similar anchoring function. Rhizoids are found in fungi and some plants, but not in seaweeds. Air spaces in tissues and lungs are not adaptive features of seaweeds.

questions.question: Nutrient ______ refers to the movement of certain nutrients
questions.explanation: Nutrient cycling refers to the movement of certain nutrients. It is the process by which nutrients move through an ecosystem. The nutrients are used by organisms, released into the environment, and then reused. It is an essential process for the survival of ecosystems.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a form of asexual reproduction?
questions.explanation: All the options listed are forms of asexual reproduction except 'Binary Fussion'. It seems like a misspelling of 'Binary Fission', which is a form of asexual reproduction.

questions.question: ______ be defined as a biochemical activity of the cell in which glucose is broken down in a series of reaction controlled by enzymes to release energy.
questions.explanation: The process described in the question is respiration. Respiration is a biochemical process in which cells of an organism obtain energy by combining oxygen and glucose, resulting in the release of carbon dioxide, water, and ATP (adenosine triphosphate). ATP is the energy currency of the cell. The other options, transpiration, perspiration, and excretion, do not involve the breakdown of glucose to release energy.

questions.question: Reptiles have scales for
questions.explanation: Reptiles have scales primarily for skin protection. The scales serve as a protective barrier against predators and environmental factors.

questions.question: The process by which manufactured food substances are transported through phloem tissue from site of production to plant tissues where they are used is called
questions.explanation: The process by which manufactured food substances are transported through phloem tissue from the site of production to plant tissues where they are used is called Translocation. It is a part of the plant's transport system, which moves water and nutrients from the roots to the rest of the plant.

questions.question: Kingdom Plantae has the following characteristics except

B.text: They are multicellular and non-motile organisms

C.text: Their cells are bounded by rigid cellulose cell walls

D.text: They are unicellular and motile organisms

questions.explanation: Kingdom Plantae consists of multicellular organisms that are typically non-motile. Their cells are bounded by rigid cellulose cell walls and they contain chlorophyll which is used for photosynthesis. They are not unicellular and motile organisms, which is a characteristic of some organisms in Kingdom Protista or Monera.

questions.question: There are ___ forms of skeletal materials found in animals
questions.explanation: There are 3 forms of skeletal materials found in animals. These are: - Chitin - Cartilage - Bones. Chitin is a hard exoskeleton that covers the bodies of insects, spiders, and other invertebrates. Cartilage is a flexible tissue that is found in the joints, between the vertebrae, and in the ears and nose. Bone is the hardest and strongest skeletal material.

questions.question: ______ is the largest organ of the body
questions.explanation: The skin is the largest organ of the body. It covers the entire body and protects the body from various environmental factors. The liver, kidney, and heart are also organs of the body, but they are not as large as the skin.

questions.question: Land pollutants includes the following except
questions.explanation: Land pollution refers to the degradation of the earth's land surfaces, often caused by human activities and their misuse of land resources. It occurs when waste is not disposed of properly, or can occur when industrial conditions such as mining cause contamination of the soil. Common land pollutants include refuse, metal scraps, and glass particles. Perfumes are not typically considered a form of land pollution.

questions.question: The theory of natural selection was developed by
questions.explanation: The theory of natural selection was developed by Charles Darwin and Alfred Russel Wallace. They independently conceived the theory of evolution through natural selection. However, Darwin's work is more widely recognized. Natural selection is the process where organisms better adapted to their environment tend to survive and produce more offspring.

questions.question: Which of the followings is not a method of conserving soil?

A.text: Prevention of overgrazing which may cause soil erosion

C.text: Prevention of indiscriminate felling of trees

questions.explanation: Soil conservation involves practices that prevent soil erosion, enhance soil fertility, and promote soil health. This typically involves techniques such as crop rotation, contour plowing, and windbreaks. Pollution of the soil is not a method of conserving soil, but rather, it is a factor that contributes to soil degradation.

questions.question: ______ is the inheritance of characteristics controlled by a single gene
questions.explanation: Monohybrid inheritance is the inheritance of characteristics controlled by a single gene. In genetics, a monohybrid cross is a breeding experiment between P generation (parental generation) organisms that differ in a single given trait.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of discontinuous variation?
questions.explanation: Discontinuous variation refers to the variation in phenotypic traits in which types are grouped into discrete categories with few or no intermediate phenotypes. Tongue rolling is an example of discontinuous variation because an individual can either roll their tongue or they can't, there is no in-between. Height, weight, and skin color are examples of continuous variation because they can vary in a population along a continuum or spectrum.

questions.question: The alternate form of a gene is
questions.explanation: In genetics, an allele is one of two or more versions of a gene that are found at the same place on a chromosome. Alleles can come in different versions, with each version bringing about different phenotypic traits. For example, a gene for flower color may have one allele that produces red flowers and another allele that produces white flowers. Therefore, the alternate form of a gene is an allele.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a behavioral adaptation for survival in animals?
questions.explanation: Migration is an example of a behavioral adaptation for survival in animals. Migration refers to the regular movement of animals from one place to another, often over long distances. Animals may migrate to find better food sources, suitable breeding grounds, or more favorable climates.

questions.question: Which gland is responsible for producing the hormone insulin?
questions.explanation: The pancreas is an organ located in the abdomen, and it serves both digestive and endocrine functions. In terms of hormone production, the pancreas is responsible for producing insulin. Insulin plays a crucial role in regulating blood sugar levels by facilitating the uptake of glucose into cells.

questions.question: Which of the following is an evolutionary trend commonly observed in organisms?
questions.explanation: Evolutionary trends generally involve changes that occur over time in a population or species. One commonly observed trend is an increase in genetic diversity within populations. Genetic diversity refers to the variety of genetic traits and variations present within a population. Increased genetic diversity can provide a population with a greater potential to adapt to changes in the environment, as it provides a wider range of genetic material for natural selection to act upon.

questions.question: What is the primary source of variation in a population?
questions.explanation: The primary source of variation in a population is mutation. Mutation is a spontaneous change in the DNA sequence of an organism's genome. It introduces new genetic variations by altering the existing genetic information.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic feature of Kingdom Plantae?
questions.explanation: Kingdom Plantae is characterized by the presence of chloroplasts. Chloroplasts are the site of photosynthesis in plant cells, where light energy is converted into chemical energy. This is not to say that all organisms with chloroplasts belong to the Plantae kingdom, but it is a common feature of this kingdom.

questions.question: Which processes are involved in nutrient cycling in a functioning ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen fixation: This process involves the conversion of atmospheric nitrogen gas (N2) into a usable form, such as ammonium (NH4+), by nitrogen-fixing bacteria or through lightning. This makes nitrogen available for plants and other organisms. Denitrification: Denitrifying bacteria convert nitrates (NO3-) back into atmospheric nitrogen gas (N2), completing the nitrogen cycle. Denitrification occurs under anaerobic conditions, such as in waterlogged soils or sediments. Ammonification: Ammonification is the process by which decomposers, like bacteria and fungi, break down organic nitrogen compounds from dead organisms and waste products into ammonium (NH4+). This ammonium can then be used by plants and other organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of physiological variation in organisms?

A.text: Variation in beak shape among finches.

B.text: Variation in blood pressure among individuals.

C.text: Differences in fur color in rabbits.

D.text: Variation in leaf shape in plants.

questions.explanation: Physiological variation refers to differences in physiological traits or functions among individuals within a species. Blood pressure is a physiological parameter that can vary among individuals based on factors such as genetics, health conditions, lifestyle, and environmental influences. Physiological variation encompasses variations in functions, processes, and internal characteristics of organisms, such as metabolic rates, hormone levels, enzyme activities, blood parameters, and other physiological traits.

questions.question: The natural place of an organism or community is known as
questions.explanation: The natural place of an organism or community is known as its habitat. A habitat is an ecological or environmental area that is inhabited by a particular species of animal, plant, or other type of organism. It is the natural environment in which an organism lives, or the physical environment that surrounds a species population.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the alimentary canal?
questions.explanation: The alimentary canal, also known as the digestive tract, is a long tube that extends from the mouth to the anus and is responsible for the digestion and absorption of food. The organs that are part of the alimentary canal include the mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, and large intestine. The liver produces bile, which aids in the digestion and absorption of fats, but its location is outside of the alimentary canal.

questions.question: A biome characterized by hot summer, warm winter and treeless vegetation is
questions.explanation: A biome characterized by hot summer, warm winter and treeless vegetation is the Steppe grassland. Steppe grasslands are characterized by grassy plains without trees apart from those near rivers and lakes. The summers can be hot, sometimes 40 °C (104 °F) or more, but winters are cold, with temperatures often falling below freezing.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a microorganism in action as a disease vector?
questions.explanation: A disease vector is an organism that transmits disease-causing pathogens from one host to another. Female Anopheles mosquitoes can carry and transmit the Plasmodium parasite, which causes malaria. When an infected mosquito bites a person, it injects the parasite into the bloodstream, leading to the transmission of the disease.

questions.question: Which organs are part of the alimentary canal in the human digestive system?
questions.explanation: The alimentary canal, also known as the gastrointestinal tract, is a long, continuous tube that extends from the mouth to the anus. It is responsible for the digestion and absorption of nutrients. Oesophagus: The esophagus is a muscular tube that connects the mouth to the stomach. Pancreas: The pancreas is both an endocrine and exocrine gland. From the digestive system perspective, it plays an important role in producing and secreting digestive enzymes into the small intestine. Small intestine: The small intestine is the longest part of the alimentary canal and is divided into three sections: duodenum, jejunum, and ileum.

questions.question: The theory of evolution can be defined as

A.text: The hypothesis that organisms strive to improve themselves over generations.

B.text: The concept that evolution occurs through a series of sudden and dramatic changes.

C.text: The belief that all species were created in their current form.

D.text: The idea that species change over time through natural processes.

questions.explanation: The theory of evolution is a scientific explanation for the diversity of life on Earth. It states that species change over time through a process known as natural selection. This theory, proposed by Charles Darwin and Alfred Russel Wallace in the 19th century, suggests that populations of organisms evolve over generations in response to changes in their environment.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the maximum number of individuals of a species that an environment can support indefinitely?
questions.explanation: Carrying capacity refers to the maximum number of individuals of a species that a particular environment can sustain over a prolonged period of time. It represents the balance between the available resources in the environment and the population's demand for those resources.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the heart is true?

A.text: The heart is a muscular organ that contracts to circulate blood throughout the body.

B.text: The heart pumps oxygenated blood to the lungs.

C.text: The heart is responsible for the production of red blood cells.

D.text: The heart receives blood from the kidneys and filters waste products.

questions.explanation: The heart is a vital organ in the circulatory system responsible for pumping and circulating blood throughout the body.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an abiotic ecological factor?
questions.explanation: Abiotic ecological factors are non-living factors that influence an ecosystem. They include physical and chemical factors such as temperature, sunlight, water availability, soil composition, and air quality.

questions.question: Which component of blood is responsible for carrying oxygen to the body tissues?
questions.explanation: Red blood cells, also known as erythrocytes, are responsible for carrying oxygen to the body tissues. They contain a protein called hemoglobin, which binds to oxygen in the lungs and transports it to other parts of the body.

questions.question: Which of the following soil types becomes less fertile due to the intense leaching caused by tropical rains?
questions.explanation: Laterite soil becomes less fertile due to intense leaching caused by tropical rains. Leaching is the process by which nutrients are washed out of the soil by rainfall. In the case of laterite soils, which are common in tropical regions, the heavy rainfall can cause intense leaching, leading to a loss of fertility.

questions.question: Which of the following traits is not visible in a person with Down syndrome?
questions.explanation: People with Down syndrome always have intellectual or physical disabilities. High muscle tone is not visible in a person with Down syndrome.

questions.question:
Most fishes do not sink in water because of the presences of:
I. swim bladder
II. air bladder
III. air sacs
IV. air in spongy bones

D.text: I, II, III and IV are correct

questions.explanation: Fishes have a swim bladder or air bladder which helps them to remain buoyant without sinking in water. They are present in the body cavity.

questions.question: The term cell was given by
questions.explanation: The term cell was given by Robert Hooke. The term cell was coined by Robert Hooke. Robert Hooke observed a honeycomb-like structure under the microscope while cutting a thin section of cork, hence he named the structure as a cell.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary organ involved in gas exchange during respiration in humans?
questions.explanation: The primary organ involved in gas exchange during respiration in humans is the lungs. The lungs are a pair of respiratory organs located in the chest cavity. They are responsible for the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide between the air and the bloodstream.

questions.question: What is autotrophic nutrition?

A.text: Nutrition in which organisms obtain food by consuming both plants and animals.

B.text: Nutrition in which organisms obtain food by breaking down complex organic compounds.

C.text: Nutrition in which organisms obtain food from other organisms.

D.text: Nutrition in which organisms produce their own food using energy from the sun or inorganic substances.

questions.explanation: Autotrophic nutrition refers to the ability of organisms to synthesize their own food molecules from simple inorganic substances such as carbon dioxide and water.

questions.question: What are the primary products of photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: The primary products of photosynthesis are glucose and oxygen. During photosynthesis, plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce glucose, a simple sugar that serves as the main source of energy for the plant. Oxygen is also produced as a byproduct of photosynthesis and is released into the atmosphere.

questions.question: Which of the following functions is performed by the skin to help maintain homeostasis in the human body?
questions.explanation: The skin plays a vital role in maintaining homeostasis by regulating body temperature. When the body becomes too hot, the skin helps to cool it down through processes like sweating and vasodilation.

questions.question: Behavioral adaptation for dealing with a hot climate could include:

A.text: Large scales on the back of a lizard

B.text: Feeding during the hottest part of the day

C.text: A small kidney to conserve water

D.text: Hibernating during the hottest part of the day

questions.explanation: Estivation: Certain animals enter a state of estivation, which is similar to hibernation but occurs in response to hot and dry conditions. They become inactive, conserve energy, and reduce water loss during the hottest parts of the day or during extended periods of high temperatures.

questions.question: What is the tissue responsible for transporting water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant?
questions.explanation: The tissue responsible for transporting water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant is called xylem. Xylem is a complex tissue composed of specialized cells, such as tracheids and vessel elements, which form long, interconnected tubes. These tubes facilitate the upward movement of water and minerals through the plant. Xylem also provides mechanical support to the plant.

questions.question: Which of the following is evidence of evolution?
questions.explanation: All the options provided are evidence of evolution, which supports the idea that species have evolved and changed over time.

questions.question: Which of the following processes is involved in the reproduction of developing organisms?
questions.explanation: Fertilization is the process by which the male and female gametes (reproductive cells) fuse to initiate the development of a new organism.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an adaptation for survival in social insects?
questions.explanation: Social insects, such as ants, bees, wasps, and termites, exhibit complex social structures and behaviors that contribute to their survival and success as a colony. Social insects organize themselves into specialized groups or castes, each with specific roles and functions. These castes can include workers, soldiers, drones, and queens. This division of labor and specialization enhances the efficiency of tasks within the colony, promoting survival and reproductive success.

questions.question: Which type of reproduction involves the fusion of gametes from two parents?
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of gametes from two parents, typically a male and a female. In this process, specialized cells called gametes (sperm and egg) are produced through meiosis. The fusion of these gametes during fertilization leads to the formation of a zygote, which develops into a new individual with a combination of genetic traits from both parents.

questions.question: The membrane around the vacuole is known as
questions.explanation: The membrane around the vacuole is known as Tonoplast. The tonoplast is the cytoplasmic membrane surrounding a vacuole, separating the vacuolar contents from the cell's cytoplasm. It is also known as Vacuolar membrane.

questions.question: What is the definition of population ecology?

A.text: The study of interactions between different populations in an ecosystem.

B.text: The study of interactions between organisms and their physical environment.

C.text: The study of the distribution and abundance of individuals within a species.

D.text: The study of evolutionary processes and their effects on populations.

questions.explanation: Population ecology is a branch of ecology that focuses on the study of populations of organisms belonging to the same species. It involves examining how populations are distributed across different habitats and how their numbers change over time.

questions.question: Which of the following is a male reproductive organ in humans?
questions.explanation: The testis is a male reproductive organ in humans. It is responsible for producing sperm cells and secreting the male sex hormone testosterone. The testes are located within the scrotum, outside the abdominal cavity.

questions.question: Which of the following represents an example of ecological management and conservation through a biological association?

A.text: Introduction of an invasive species for pest control.

C.text: Clear-cutting of a forest for timber extraction.

D.text: Construction of a dam for hydroelectric power.

questions.explanation: Ecological management and conservation involve strategies and practices aimed at preserving and protecting natural ecosystems and their biodiversity. Among the options provided, the establishment of marine protected areas is an example of such management and conservation through a biological association.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of cells related to irritability?
questions.explanation: Irritability, also known as responsiveness or excitability, refers to the ability of cells to detect and respond to various stimuli or changes in their environment.

questions.question: Which process in the nutrient cycle converts atmospheric nitrogen into a form that plants can utilize?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen fixation is the process in the nutrient cycle that converts atmospheric nitrogen (N2) into a form that plants can utilize, typically ammonium (NH4+) or nitrate (NO3-). This process is carried out by nitrogen-fixing bacteria, which can either be free-living in the soil or form mutualistic relationships with certain plants, such as legumes.

questions.question: In monohybrid inheritance, if an organism carries two different alleles for a particular gene, it is called:
questions.explanation: In monohybrid inheritance, the inheritance of a single gene is considered. An organism that carries two different alleles for a particular gene is referred to as heterozygous. The alleles may have different versions or variations of the gene. Heterozygous individuals carry two different alleles, with one being dominant and the other recessive.

questions.question: Which of the following are components of the skeletal system in humans?
questions.explanation: The skeletal system in humans is composed of bones and joints. Bones and joints are the primary components of the human skeletal system.

questions.question: Consider the reaction: \( 2Al_{(s)} + 6H^+_{(aq)} \rightarrow 2Al^{3+}_{(aq)} + 3H_2_{(g)} \). What is the total number of moles of electrons transferred from the aluminium atoms to the hydrogen ions?
questions.explanation:
Equation of the reaction, \( 2Al + 6H^+ \rightarrow 2Al^{3+} + 3H_2 \)
\( 2Al - 2[3e^-] \rightarrow 2Al^{3+} \)
\( 2Al \rightarrow 2Al^{3+} + 6e^- \)
6 moles of electrons is transferred to Hydrogen ions.

questions.question:
\( N_2(g) + 3H_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2NH_3(g) \)
What role does the finely divided iron play in the Haber process of manufacturing ammonia?
questions.explanation: In the Haber process, finely divided iron acts as a catalyst. A catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without itself undergoing any permanent chemical change.

questions.question: The oxidation number of chlorine is 1 in
questions.explanation: The oxidation number of chlorine in NaClO (Sodium hypochlorite) is +1. Oxidation number is a concept in chemistry that helps in understanding the oxidation and reduction reactions. It is the charge that an atom would have if the compound was composed of ions.

questions.question: What do polymorphs have in common?
questions.explanation: Polymorphism is the ability of a solid material to exist in two or more forms or crystalline structures of the same chemical compound and it is a solid state phenomena. Polymorphism can found in any crystalline material like polymers, minerals, and metals. They may differ in physical appearance and properties, but would have the same chemical properties.

questions.question: The ability of an atom to attract electrons towards itself is called
questions.explanation: The property of an atom which determines its ability to attract electrons to itself in a bond is called electronegativity. Electron affinity is the amount of energy released when one mole of electron is added to a non-metal (in the gaseous state) to form an anion (also in the gaseous state).

questions.question: The minimum amount of energy that colliding molecules must reach or possess for their collisions to be successfully known as the
questions.explanation: The minimum amount of energy that colliding molecules must reach or possess for their collisions to be successful is known as the activation energy. It is the energy barrier that must be overcome for a chemical reaction to occur.

questions.question: Chemical equilibrium is based on …..

A.text: The rate of two opposing reactions

B.text: The equality of the forward and reverse reactions

C.text: The equality of the rates of the forward and reverse reaction

D.text: The conditions of two opposing reactions

questions.explanation: In a reversible system, when the chemical equilibrium state is reached, the rate of forward reaction is equal to the rate of backward reaction.

questions.question: During the electrolysis of a caustic pottash solution using platinum electrodes

A.text: potassium metal is deposited at the anode

B.text: oxygen gas is given off at the cathode

D.text: potassium metal is deposited at the cathode

questions.explanation: At the cathode, \( H^+ \), being lower in the activity series discharges in preference to \( K^+ \), Hydrogen gas is produced at this electrode. The discharge of \( H^+ \) at this electrode disturbs the ionic equilibrium of water \( (H_2O_{(l)}) \rightleftharpoons H^+_{(aq)} + OH^-_{(aq)} \) hence, excess \( OH^- \) ions are produced. That is alkalinity increases at this electrode.

questions.question: Which of these chemical species has no neutron in the nucleus of its atom?
questions.explanation: The hydrogen atom is the only chemical species that has no neutron in the nucleus of its atom. The nucleus of a hydrogen atom consists of only one proton.

questions.question: A likely consequence of using fuels with high sulphur content is
questions.explanation: When fuel with Sulphur content is burned, it produces SO₂ which combines with rain water to form trioxosulphate (iv) and tetraoxosulphate (vi) acid, hence acid rain.

questions.question:
In the reaction below, the substance that will increase the rate of production of Ammonia is
\( N_2(g) + 3H_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2NH_3(g) \)
questions.explanation: In the Haber process for the production of ammonia (\(N_2(g) + 3H_2(g) \rightleftharpoons 2NH_3(g)\)), finely divided iron is used as a catalyst to increase the rate of the reaction. A catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction by providing an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy. It does not change the position of the equilibrium or the energy of the reactants or products.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is lighter than air?
questions.explanation: NH₃ (Ammonia) is lighter than air. Ammonia has a density of \( 0.73 \, \text{kg/m}^3 \) whereas the density of air at sea level is \( 1.225 \, \text{kg/m}^3 \).

questions.question:
At 27°C, 300cm³ of a gas sample exerts a pressure of 220mmHg. How many molecules are present in the gas sample?
\( (N_A = 6.02 \times 10^{23}, R = 0.0821 \, \text{atm} \, \text{dm}^3 \, \text{mol}^{-1} \, \text{k}^{-1}) \)
questions.explanation:
From the Ideal gas equation \( n = \frac{PV}{RT} \)
number of mole \( n = ? \)
\( P = \text{Pressure} = \frac{720}{760} \, \text{atm} = 0.947 \)
\( v = \text{volume of gases} = \frac{300}{1000} \, \text{dm}^3 = 0.3 \, \text{dm}^3 \)
\( T = (27 + 273) \, \text{K} = 300 \, \text{K} \)
\( R = 0.0821 \, \text{atm dm}^3 \cdot \text{mol}^{-1} \cdot \text{K}^{-1} \)
\( n = \frac{0.947 \times 0.3}{300 \, \text{mol}} \)
\( n = 0.0115 \, \text{mole} \)
1 mole of the gas contains \( 6.02 \times 10^{23} \) molecules
\( 0.0115 \, \text{mole} \) of gas would contain \( (0.0115 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}) \)
\( = 6.9 \times 10^{21} \, \text{molecules} \)

questions.question: Which of the following ammonium salt is used in the manufacture of dry cells?
questions.explanation: \( NH_4Cl \) (in form of paste or jelly mixed with starch or flour serves as the electrolytes in dry cells e.g Leclanche cell.

questions.question: Metals can be extracted from their ores by a process involving
questions.explanation: Metals are typically extracted from their ores through a process called reduction. This involves the removal of oxygen from the metal oxide (ore) to yield the pure metal. Hence, the correct option is 1.

questions.question: What do polymorphs have in common?
questions.explanation: Polymorphism is the ability of a solid material to exist in two or more forms or crystalline structures of the same chemical compound and it is a solid state phenomena. Polymorphism can found in any crystalline material like polymers, minerals, and metals. They may differ in physical appearance and properties, but would have the same chemical properties.

questions.question: An oxide which is reduced when heated in a stream of hydrogen is
questions.explanation: Hydrogen cannot reduce oxides of metals that stand high in the activity series but can reduce those that are moderately reactive.

questions.question: A mixture of chalk and water can be separated by
questions.explanation: A mixture of chalk and water can be separated by filtration. Filtration is a mechanical or physical operation which is used for the separation of solids from fluids (liquids or gases) by interposing a medium through which only the fluid can pass.

questions.question: An advantage of detergents over soaps is that detergents
questions.explanation: Detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water. Hard water contains ions that react with soap to form a precipitate, which is not useful for cleaning. Detergents, on the other hand, do not form this precipitate and thus lather readily with water.

questions.question: The Inter atomic bonds present in the sodium trioxocarbonate (IV) compound are
questions.explanation: Electrovalent bond between sodium ion and carbonate ion and covalent bonds in the carbonate ions (\( \text{CO}_3^{2-} \))

questions.question: Which of the following substances will decompose when an electric current is passed through it.
questions.explanation: It is an electrolyte that decomposes when an electrical current is passed through it. The only electrolyte among the options is tetraoxosulphate (VI) acid.

questions.question: Which of the following reagent will remove \( \text{CO}_2 \) from a mixture of \( \text{CO}_2 \) and \( \text{N}_2 \) is
questions.explanation: Aqueous KOH (Potassium hydroxide) is used to remove \( \text{CO}_2 \) from a mixture of \( \text{CO}_2 \) and \( \text{N}_2 \). This is because KOH reacts with \( \text{CO}_2 \) to form potassium carbonate and water, effectively removing the \( \text{CO}_2 \) from the mixture.

questions.question: A physical change is
questions.explanation: Dissolution of sugar in water is a reversible process. The sugar can be recovered from sugar solution by evaporation to dryness or by evaporation to dryness or by crystallization. Hence, the process is a physical change.

questions.question:
What is the value of \( x \) in \( \text{Fe}(\text{NH}_4)_2(\text{SO}_4)_2 \cdot \text{XH}_2\text{O} \) if molar mass is 392 gmol\(^{-1}\)?
\( [\text{Fe} = 56, \, N = 14, \, S = 32, \, O = 16, \, H = 1] \)

| A |   |   |   | B |   |   |   |
| C |   | D |   |   |   |   |   |
| E |   |   | F | G |   |   |   |

Which of the letters indicates elements which exists as diatomic gases?
questions.explanation: Diatomic gases from the table given are the elements in group 7 and group 1.

questions.question: The solubility of alkanols in water is due to
questions.explanation:
The solubility of alkanols in water is due to the presence of hydroxyl group and which contributes hydrogen bonding.
R - OH

questions.question: Which of the following gases can best be collected by downward displacement of air?
questions.explanation: Ammonia is lighter than air and hence can be collected by downward displacement of air. This means that the ammonia gas will rise and displace the air downwards.

questions.question:
What is the empirical formula of an organic compound containing 40.1% C and 6.6% H?
(C = 12, H = 1, O = 16)
questions.explanation:
% Comp. of Carbon = 40.1
% Comp. of Hydrogen = 6.6
sum of C and H = 46.7%
% comp. of unknown element or oxygen = \( (100 - 46.7)\% = 53.3% \)

|    | C   | H   | O   |
|---|---|---|---|
| **%Comp by mass** | 40.1 | 6.6  | 53.3 |
| **No. of moles**  | 40.1/12 = 3.3 | 6.6/1 = 6.6 | 53.3/16 = 3.3 |
| **Divide by smallest (3.3)** | 1 | 2 | 1 |

The empirical formula of the organic compound \( CH_2O \)
This is glucose.

questions.question: When air is successfully passed through sodium hydroxide solution, alkaline, pyragallol and then concentrated tetraoxosulphate (VI), its remaining components are
questions.explanation:
Air contains \( CO_2 \), \( O_2 \), Water vapour, rare gases and Nitrogen in sufficient amount.
When air is passed through sodium hydroxide solution, alkaline pyrogallol and conc. \( H_2SO_4 \), \( CO_2 \), \( O_2 \) and water vapour are removed respectively. Therefore, we are left with Nitrogen and rare gases.

questions.question: Which among these has neither charge nor mass?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have neither charge nor mass. They are a form of electromagnetic radiation, similar to light or radio waves, and as such, they are massless and carry no charge.

questions.question: The Solubility of \( \text{KHCO}_3 \) is 0.42 mol/dm\(^3\) at 25°. What is the mass of \( \text{KHCO}_3 \) in 200 cm\(^3\) of a saturated solution of this salt at 25°C? [\( \text{KHCO}_3 = 100 \, \text{gmol}^{-1} \)]
questions.explanation:
The solubility value (0.42 mol/dm\(^3\)) indicates that: 1000 cm\(^3\) of solution contains 0.42 mol/dm\(^3\) \( \text{KHCO}_3 \) therefore 200 cm\(^3\) of solution would contain \((0.42 \times \frac{200}{1000}) \, \text{mol} = 0.084 \, \text{mol KHCO}_3\)

mass of \( \text{KHCO}_3 \) = Mole × Molar mass
\( = (0.084 \times 100) = 8.4 \, \text{g} \)

questions.question:
What is the percentage composition of Oxygen in \( K_2Cr_2O_7 \)?
\( [K_2Cr_2O_7 = 294, O = 16, K = 39, Cr = 52] \)
Which among these is a "fine" chemical?
questions.explanation: "Fine" chemical is one that is not commonly used on daily basis. Silver bromide is specially used in the photographic industry while the other chemicals are commonly used in the laboratory.

questions.question: Chlorine gas is best collected
questions.explanation: Chlorine gas is denser than air hence its collection by downward delivery (upward displacement of air).

questions.question: Which among these is generated during the manufacturing of fertilizers?
questions.explanation: Tetraoxosulphate (VI) acid is a primary raw material in the fertilizing industry. Sulphur(IV)oxide is produced in the Process of forming \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) from sulphur. It is also a by-product in the reaction of phosphate rock-with \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) in the production of superphosphate fertilizer.

questions.question: Which among these is generated during the manufacturing of fertilizers?
questions.explanation: Tetraoxosulphate (VI) acid is a primary raw material in the fertilizing industry. Sulphur(IV)oxide is produced in the Process of forming \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) from sulphur. It is also a by-product in the reaction of phosphate rock-with \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) in the production of superphosphate fertilizer.
